content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Search Protocol
66	
67	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
68	
69	1. `lean_local_search` first
70	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
71	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
72	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
73	
74	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
75	
76	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
77	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
78	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
79	
80	## Logging
81	
82	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
83	
84	**Format:**
85	
86	```markdown
87	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
88	
89	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
90	### Attempt 1
91	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
92	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
93	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
94	
95	### Attempt 2
96	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
97	- **Result:** RESOLVED
98	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
99	
100	## helper_bijective (line 78)
101	### Attempt 1
102	- **Approach:** Function.Bijective.comp
103	- **Result:** IN PROGRESS — stuck on surjectivity
104	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective
105	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
106	```
107	
108	**Rules:**
109	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
110	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
111	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
112	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
113	
114	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
115	
116	## Summary Pipeline
117	
118	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
119	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
120	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
121	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
122	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
123	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
124	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
125	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
130	
131	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
132	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
133	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
134	   - Concrete next step for the next session
135	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
136	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)
137

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	
5	prover (iteration 072 — four parallel lanes).
6	
7	## Stages
8	- [x] init
9	- [x] autoformalize
10	- [ ] prover
11	- [ ] polish
12	
13	## Big-picture context
14	
15	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
16	
17	`STRATEGY.md` describes the five-phase build-out plan. After iter-071's three-track prover round (−7 net sorries, the largest single-iter delta since iter-064), the sorry landscape is:
18	
19	| File | Sorries | Phase | Status |
20	|---|---|---|---|
21	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` | **6** | A | iter-072 Lane 4 |
22	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` | **8** | B | iter-072 Lane 3 |
23	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` | **1** | C/D (consolidated) | iter-072 Lane 1 (refactor) |
24	| `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` | 3 | E (now unblocked) | iter-072 Lane 2 |
25	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` | 1 | C-rep (deferred) | off-limits |
26	| **Total** | **19** | | |
27	
28	Iter-071's structural highlight was the `JacobianWitness` single-witness consolidation pattern on `Jacobian.lean`, which collapsed five originally-protected sorries into one unprotected existence hypothesis. This unblocks Phase E at the type-checking level — `Jacobian C` is now well-typed for both genus branches, so `AbelJacobi.lean`'s three sorries are ready for closure.
29	
30	## Current Objectives
31	
32	The dispatcher fans out one prover per file listed below. Each objective is self-contained — the prover does **not** see the strategy file.
33	
34	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` — Phase C consolidation refactor (precursor to Lane 2)
35	
36	**File status.** 1 syntactic sorry, 228 LOC. Theorem under attack: `nonempty_jacobianWitness` (L162). Iter-071 prover bundled the five originally-protected sorries (`Jacobian` g>0 branch, `instGrpObj`, `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`, `instGeometricallyIrreducible`) into a single existence hypothesis via the `JacobianWitness` structure, with the four protected instances projecting the relevant field of `(jacobianWitness C)`.
37	
38	**Iter-072 target.** **Refactor — do not attempt to close the existence sorry**. The `JacobianWitness` structure currently fixes an internal marked point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C` as a field, with `isAlbanese : @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred` indexed on that internal `P`. This is incompatible with `AbelJacobi.Jacobian.ofCurve P` (whose `P` is an arbitrary input). Refactor as follows:
39	
40	1. **Remove the `P` field from `JacobianWitness`.** The bundle should no longer carry a marked point.
41	2. **Parameterise the `isAlbanese` field on `P`**:
42	   ```
43	   isAlbaneseFor : ∀ (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C), @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred
44	   ```
45	   so that the same Albanese object `J` serves as Albanese for every choice of pointing. This is mathematically correct: the *scheme* `J` is intrinsic; only the universal morphism depends on `P`.
46	3. **`nonempty_jacobianWitness` is unchanged in signature** (`Nonempty (JacobianWitness C) := sorry`), since `P` is no longer a parameter of the bundle.
47	4. **`jacobianWitness C` unchanged** (`Classical.choice (nonempty_jacobianWitness C)`).
48	5. **The four protected instances are unchanged in proof** — each still projects the relevant field of `jacobianWitness C` (which no longer has a `P` field but still has `grpObj`, `proper`, `smooth`, `geomIrred`, `smoothGenus`, `J`).
49	6. **`Jacobian C` unchanged** — its g>0 branch still projects `(jacobianWitness C).J`.
50	
51	Verify the single remaining sorry is `nonempty_jacobianWitness` and that all four protected instances still discharge. Document the new shape in your task result so Lane 2 can rely on it.
52	
53	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Jacobian.tex` (§ *The Albanese universal property*, *The Jacobian definition*, *Existence of an Albanese variety*).
54	
55	**Hard constraints.** Protected signatures (`Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`, `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`, `instGeometricallyIrreducible`) frozen. No new axioms. **Do not attempt `nonempty_jacobianWitness`** — it is the deferred Phase C existence claim (no symmetric powers, no FGA representability in current Mathlib).
56	
57	### 2. `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` — Phase E unblocked
58	
59	**File status.** 3 syntactic sorries, 57 LOC. Three protected declarations, all bare `:= sorry` / `by sorry`:
60	
61	| Decl | Line | Type |
62	|---|---|---|
63	| `Jacobian.ofCurve` | L34 | `(P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C) → C ⟶ Jacobian C` |
64	| `Jacobian.comp_ofCurve` | L39 | `P ≫ ofCurve P = η[Jacobian C]` |
65	| `Jacobian.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` | L49 | Albanese factorisation existence + uniqueness |
66	
67	**Iter-072 target.** Close all three sorries via case-split on `genus C = 0` + witness projection. **Precondition**: this iteration's Lane 1 (`Jacobian.lean` prover) is concurrently refactoring `JacobianWitness` to parameterise `isAlbanese` on `P`. Write your code against the **target post-refactor shape**:
68	
69	- `JacobianWitness C` has no `P` field.
70	- `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P : IsAlbanese C P (jacobianWitness C).J` — a forall-indexed Albanese predicate.
71	- The four protected instances on `Jacobian C` are independent of the choice of `P`.
72	
73	If Lane 1's refactor has not yet landed when your edits compile, your edits will reference field/method names that do not exist; the compile error will be informative and iter-073 will resolve. The architectural decision (parameterised `isAlbaneseFor`) is fixed.
74	
75	**Strategy per declaration.**
76	
77	- **`ofCurve P`** (L34). Two cases:

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian
7	
8	/-!
9	# The Abel-Jacobi map
10	
11	The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian, and the universal property
12	of the Jacobian as the Albanese variety.
13	-/
14	
15	set_option autoImplicit false
16	
17	universe u
18	
19	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
20	
21	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
22	
23	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
24	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
25	  [IsProper C.hom]
26	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
27	
28	namespace Jacobian
29	
30	variable {C} in
31	-- data
32	/-- The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian associated
33	to a `k`-rational point of `C`. -/
34	def ofCurve (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) : C ⟶ Jacobian C :=
35	  sorry
36	
37	/-- The Abel-Jacobi map sends the `k`-rational point `P` to `0`, where `0` (denoted by `η` below) is
38	the neutral element of the group scheme `Jacobian C`. -/
39	lemma comp_ofCurve (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) :
40	    P ≫ ofCurve P = η[Jacobian C] :=
41	  sorry
42	
43	/--
44	The universal property of the Jacobian variety: For any abelian variety `A`,
45	any morphism `f : C ⟶ A` such that `f(P) = 0` factors uniquely through the
46	Jacobian of `C`.
47	In other words, `Jacobian C` is the Albanese variety of `C`.
48	-/
49	theorem exists_unique_ofCurve_comp (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
50	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [Smooth A.hom] [IsProper A.hom] [GrpObj A]
51	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
52	    ∃! (g : Jacobian C ⟶ A), f = ofCurve P ≫ g :=
53	  sorry
54	
55	end Jacobian
56	
57	end AlgebraicGeometry
58

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus
7	
8	/-! # The Jacobian of a smooth proper curve
9	
10	The Jacobian of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field, equipped with
11	its structure as an abelian variety.
12	
13	## Status (iteration 069 — Phase C scaffolding)
14	
15	The genus-0 case is closed: `Jacobian C = 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))` (the terminal object),
16	which is `Spec k` with its identity morphism. For `g > 0`, the definition reduces to the
17	Albanese construction (deferred).
18	
19	The file now contains:
20	- `IsAlbanese`: the Albanese universal property for a pointed curve.
21	- `IsAlbanese.unique`: uniqueness of the Albanese object up to canonical isomorphism.
22	- Decomposition of the five protected sorries into genus-0 / genus-pos cases.
23	
24	### Forbidden shortcut (sanity check)
25	
26	Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))` *unconditionally* (the terminal object,
27	i.e. `Spec k`) compiles and discharges three of the four instances for free, but the
28	fourth instance `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (𝟙_ _).hom` would force `genus C = 0`,
29	which is mathematically wrong for any curve of genus `≥ 1`. The terminal-object definition
30	is therefore forbidden when `genus C > 0`.
31	
32	The iteration-069 resolution is a *conditional* use of the terminal object: it is used
33	exactly when `genus C = 0`, which is the mathematically correct answer for that case.
34	-/
35	
36	set_option autoImplicit false
37	
38	universe u
39	
40	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
41	
42	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
43	
44	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
45	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
46	  [IsProper C.hom]
47	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
48	
49	/-- The Albanese universal property for a pointed smooth proper curve `(C, P)`.
50	An object `J` (with abelian-variety structure) is the Albanese of `(C, P)` if it
51	receives a universal pointed morphism `C ⟶ J` sending `P` to the identity. -/
52	def IsAlbanese (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C) (J : Over (Spec (.of k)))
53	    [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] : Prop :=
54	  ∃ (ι : C ⟶ J), P ≫ ι = η[J] ∧
55	    ∀ {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom]
56	      [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]),
57	      ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = ι ≫ g
58	
59	namespace IsAlbanese
60	
61	noncomputable def ofCurve {C P J} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
62	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) : C ⟶ J :=
63	  Classical.choose h
64	
65	lemma comp_ofCurve {C P J} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
66	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) :
67	    P ≫ h.ofCurve = η[J] :=
68	  (Classical.choose_spec h).1
69	
70	lemma exists_unique_ofCurve_comp {C P J} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
71	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J)
72	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
73	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
74	    ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = h.ofCurve ≫ g :=
75	  (Classical.choose_spec h).2 f hf
76	
77	/-- The Albanese object is unique up to a unique isomorphism compatible with the
78	universal morphisms. -/
79	theorem unique {C : Over (Spec (.of k))} {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C}
80	    {J₁ J₂ : Over (Spec (.of k))}
81	    [GrpObj J₁] [IsProper J₁.hom] [Smooth J₁.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₁.hom]
82	    [GrpObj J₂] [IsProper J₂.hom] [Smooth J₂.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₂.hom]
83	    (h₁ : IsAlbanese C P J₁) (h₂ : IsAlbanese C P J₂) :
84	    ∃! (e : J₁ ⟶ J₂), h₂.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ e := by
85	  have ⟨g, hg_eq, hg_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
86	  have ⟨h, hh_eq, hh_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
87	  have ⟨g₁, hg₁_eq, hg₁_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
88	  have g₁_eq_id : g₁ = 𝟙 J₁ := by
89	    refine (hg₁_unique (𝟙 J₁) ?_).symm
90	    simp
91	  have ggh_eq_g₁ : g ≫ h = g₁ := by
92	    refine (hg₁_unique (g ≫ h) ?_).symm
93	    rw [Category.assoc, hg_eq, hh_eq]
94	  have hgh : g ≫ h = 𝟙 J₁ := by rw [ggh_eq_g₁, g₁_eq_id]
95	  have ⟨h₂, hh₂_eq, hh₂_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
96	  have h₂_eq_id : h₂ = 𝟙 J₂ := by
97	    refine (hh₂_unique (𝟙 J₂) ?_).symm
98	    simp
99	  have hhg_eq_h₂ : h ≫ g = h₂ := by
100	    refine (hh₂_unique (h ≫ g) ?_).symm
101	    rw [Category.assoc, hh_eq, hg_eq]
102	  have hhg : h ≫ g = 𝟙 J₂ := by rw [hhg_eq_h₂, h₂_eq_id]
103	  use g
104	  constructor
105	  · exact hg_eq
106	  · intro g' hg'
107	    exact (hg_unique g' hg').symm
108	
109	end IsAlbanese
110	
111	/-- The identity morphism on `Spec k` is geometrically irreducible.
112	This is a small helper needed for the genus-0 case of `Jacobian`. -/
113	lemma geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec (k : Type u) [Field k] :
114	    GeometricallyIrreducible (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))) := by
115	  constructor
116	  intro x K _ y Z fst snd h
117	  have : IsIso snd := h.isIso_snd_of_isIso
118	  rw [ObjectProperty.prop_iff_of_iso (IrreducibleSpace ·) (asIso snd)]
119	  infer_instance
120	
121	/-- A bundled Albanese witness for the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
122	`C`: a candidate group scheme `J` (with proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible
123	structure, smooth of relative dimension `genus C`), a marked $k$-rational point
124	`P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`, and a proof that `(J, P)` satisfies the Albanese universal property
125	of Definition `IsAlbanese` for `C`. The smoothness witness is recorded at the specific
126	relative dimension `genus C`, which is the form required by
127	`Jacobian.smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`. -/
128	structure JacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
129	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] where
130	  /-- The candidate Albanese object. -/
131	  J : Over (Spec (.of k))
132	  /-- The group-scheme structure on `J`. -/
133	  grpObj : GrpObj J
134	  /-- `J` is proper over `k`. -/
135	  proper : IsProper J.hom
136	  /-- `J` is smooth over `k`. -/
137	  smooth : Smooth J.hom
138	  /-- `J` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
139	  geomIrred : GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom
140	  /-- `J` is smooth of relative dimension `genus C` (so dim `J = genus C`). -/
141	  smoothGenus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) J.hom
142	  /-- The marked $k$-rational point of `C`. -/
143	  P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C
144	  /-- The Albanese universal-property data for `(C, P, J)`. -/
145	  isAlbanese : @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred
146	
147	/-- Existence of an Albanese witness for every smooth proper geometrically irreducible
148	curve. This is the single remaining mathematical sorry of the Phase-C Jacobian
149	scaffolding: it packages all five protected sorries (`Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`,
150	`smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`, `instGeometricallyIrreducible`)
151	into a single existence hypothesis.
152	
153	Mathematically this corresponds to the construction of the Albanese variety of `C`,
154	classically via symmetric powers $\mathrm{Sym}^g(C)$ and the Abel--Jacobi map
155	(Brill--Noether--Riemann--Roch), or equivalently as the identity component of the
156	Picard scheme. Both routes require infrastructure not yet available in Mathlib
157	(quotients of schemes by finite group actions; FGA representability), so the
158	existence is assumed and the proof is deferred to a future iteration. -/
159	theorem nonempty_jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
160	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
161	    Nonempty (JacobianWitness C) :=
162	  sorry
163	
164	/-- A choice of Albanese witness for `C`, extracted via `Classical.choice`.
165	Used to populate the genus `> 0` branch of `Jacobian C` and to discharge each of
166	the four protected instances. -/
167	noncomputable def jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
168	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
169	    JacobianWitness C :=
170	  Classical.choice (nonempty_jacobianWitness C)
171	
172	-- data
173	/-- The Jacobian of a smooth, proper curve over a field `k`.
174	
175	For genus `0`, the Jacobian is the terminal object `Spec k` (the trivial group scheme).
176	For genus `> 0`, the Jacobian is the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness for `C`
177	(see `JacobianWitness` and `nonempty_jacobianWitness`); the existence of such a
178	witness is the single remaining mathematical sorry of the Phase-C scaffolding. -/
179	noncomputable def Jacobian (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
180	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
181	  if h : genus C = 0 then
182	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))
183	  else
184	    -- Phase C, iteration 071: project to the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness
185	    -- (`JacobianWitness`). Existence of such a witness is the sole remaining
186	    -- mathematical sorry; the four protected instance fields below all reduce to
187	    -- projecting the relevant component of this witness.
188	    (jacobianWitness C).J
189	
190	namespace Jacobian
191	
192	/-! ## The Jacobian of `C` is an abelian variety. -/
193	
194	-- data
195	/-- The group scheme structure on the Jacobian of the curve `C`. -/
196	instance instGrpObj : GrpObj (Jacobian C) := by
197	  unfold Jacobian
198	  split_ifs
199	  · infer_instance
200	  · exact (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
201	
202	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is smooth of relative dimension `g` over `k`, where `g` is the
203	genus of `C`. -/
204	instance smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (Jacobian C).hom := by
205	  unfold Jacobian
206	  split_ifs with h
207	  · rw [h]
208	    infer_instance
209	  · exact (jacobianWitness C).smoothGenus
210	
211	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is proper over `k`. -/
212	instance instIsProper : IsProper (Jacobian C).hom := by
213	  unfold Jacobian
214	  split_ifs
215	  · infer_instance
216	  · exact (jacobianWitness C).proper
217	
218	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
219	instance instGeometricallyIrreducible : GeometricallyIrreducible (Jacobian C).hom := by
220	  unfold Jacobian
221	  split_ifs
222	  · exact geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec k
223	  · exact (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
224	
225	end Jacobian
226	
227	end AlgebraicGeometry

/-
Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Christian Merten
-/
import AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian

/-!
# The Abel-Jacobi map

The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian, and the universal property
of the Jacobian as the Albanese variety.

## Status (iteration 072 — Phase E unblocked)

The three protected declarations are closed via case-split on `genus C = 0`:

* `genus C = 0`: `Jacobian C = 𝟙_ _` (the terminal object). The Abel-Jacobi map is the
  unique morphism `toUnit C : C ⟶ 𝟙_ _`, the equation `P ≫ toUnit C = η[𝟙_ _]` follows from
  terminal uniqueness (`Subsingleton.elim` on `Hom(𝟙_ _, 𝟙_ _)`), and the Albanese factorisation
  takes `g := η[A]`. Uniqueness of `g` is automatic by precomposition with `P`. *Existence*
  of `g` requires the classical rigidity claim: every morphism `C ⟶ A` from a genus-0 curve
  to an abelian variety with `P ↦ η[A]` is constant at `η[A]`. This is mathematically true
  (Mumford rigidity / `Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)`) but currently unavailable in Mathlib; we leave
  a single scoped `sorry` at the rigidity step.

* `genus C > 0`: All three results reduce to the Albanese universal property carried by
  `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P : IsAlbanese C P (jacobianWitness C).J`
  (target post-refactor shape — Lane 1 of iter-072).
-/

set_option autoImplicit false

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
  [IsProper C.hom]
  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]

namespace Jacobian

variable {C} in
-- data
/-- The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian associated
to a `k`-rational point of `C`. -/
noncomputable def ofCurve (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) : C ⟶ Jacobian C := by
  unfold Jacobian
  split_ifs with h
  · -- genus C = 0 branch: Jacobian C = 𝟙_ _ (terminal). The Abel-Jacobi map is
    -- the unique morphism C ⟶ 𝟙_ _.
    exact toUnit C
  · -- genus C > 0 branch: project the Albanese universal map of the chosen
    -- witness `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`.
    letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
    letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
    letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
    letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
    exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve

variable {C} in
/-- The Abel-Jacobi map sends the `k`-rational point `P` to `0`, where `0` (denoted by `η` below) is
the neutral element of the group scheme `Jacobian C`. -/
lemma comp_ofCurve (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) :
    P ≫ ofCurve P = η[Jacobian C] := by
  -- Unfold both `ofCurve` and `Jacobian` so the dite over `genus C = 0` is exposed
  -- on both sides of the equation simultaneously.
  unfold ofCurve Jacobian
  split_ifs with h
  · -- genus C = 0 branch.
    -- After splits: P ≫ toUnit C = η[𝟙_ _] = 𝟙 (𝟙_ _).
    -- Both sides live in Hom(𝟙_ _, 𝟙_ _), which is Subsingleton.
    apply Subsingleton.elim
  · -- genus C > 0 branch: reduce to the witness's `IsAlbanese.comp_ofCurve`.
    letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
    letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
    letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
    letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
    exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).comp_ofCurve

variable {C} in
/--
The universal property of the Jacobian variety: For any abelian variety `A`,
any morphism `f : C ⟶ A` such that `f(P) = 0` factors uniquely through the
Jacobian of `C`.
In other words, `Jacobian C` is the Albanese variety of `C`.
-/
theorem exists_unique_ofCurve_comp (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [Smooth A.hom] [IsProper A.hom] [GrpObj A]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
    ∃! (g : Jacobian C ⟶ A), f = ofCurve P ≫ g := by
  unfold ofCurve Jacobian
  split_ifs with h
  · -- genus C = 0 branch.
    -- Goal: ∃! (g : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ A), f = toUnit C ≫ g.
    -- Take g := η[A]. Existence is the rigidity claim (sorry). Uniqueness follows
    -- by precomposition with P: from f = toUnit C ≫ g' we get
    --   g' = (P ≫ toUnit C) ≫ g' = P ≫ (toUnit C ≫ g') = P ≫ f = η[A],
    -- since P ≫ toUnit C : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ 𝟙_ _ is the identity (`Subsingleton.elim`).
    refine ⟨η[A], ?existence, ?uniqueness⟩
    case existence =>
      -- f = toUnit C ≫ η[A].
      -- Rigidity for genus-0 curves to abelian varieties: every morphism C ⟶ A
      -- with f ∘ P = η[A] is the constant map at η[A]. Classically true (Mumford),
      -- but Mathlib currently lacks the infrastructure (no `Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)`,
      -- no Mumford §4 rigidity theorem). Recorded as a single scoped sorry.
      sorry
    case uniqueness =>
      intro g' hg'
      -- From hg' : f = toUnit C ≫ g', precompose with P:
      --   P ≫ f = P ≫ toUnit C ≫ g' = (P ≫ toUnit C) ≫ g' = 𝟙 (𝟙_ _) ≫ g' = g'
      -- (using `Subsingleton.elim` on the terminal hom-set).
      have hPtoUnit : P ≫ toUnit C = 𝟙 (𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))) :=
        Subsingleton.elim _ _
      have : P ≫ f = g' := by
        rw [hg', ← Category.assoc, hPtoUnit, Category.id_comp]
      -- Combine with hf : P ≫ f = η[A]:
      rw [hf] at this
      exact this.symm
  · -- genus C > 0 branch: reduce to the witness's
    -- `IsAlbanese.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`.
    letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
    letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
    letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
    letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
    exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf

end Jacobian

end AlgebraicGeometry

open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj

variable {C : Type*} [Category C] [CartesianMonoidalCategory C]

-- Mock IsAlbanese-style structure
def IsAlbanese' (X J : C) (P : 𝟙_ C ⟶ X) [MonObj J] : Prop :=
  ∃ (ι : X ⟶ J), P ≫ ι = η[J] ∧
    ∀ {A : C} [MonObj A] (f : X ⟶ A) (_ : P ≫ f = η[A]),
      ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = ι ≫ g

-- Mock witness
structure JacWit (X : C) where
  J : C
  monObj : MonObj J

-- Mock Jacobian using a fictional "genus" predicate

noncomputable def jacWit (X : C) : JacWit X := sorry

noncomputable def Jac (X : C) : C :=
  if genusZero X then 𝟙_ C else (jacWit genusZero X).J

-- Test: define ofCurve via case-split on genus
variable (X : C) (P : 𝟙_ C ⟶ X)

noncomputable def ofCurveTest : X ⟶ Jac genusZero X := by
  unfold Jac
  split_ifs with h
  · exact toUnit X
  · letI := (jacWit genusZero X).monObj

-- Test: prove a composition lemma
example : P ≫ ofCurveTest genusZero X P = (sorry : 𝟙_ C ⟶ Jac genusZero X) := by
  unfold ofCurveTest Jac
  split_ifs with h
  · apply Subsingleton.elim
  · letI := (jacWit genusZero X).monObj
    sorry

import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Cartesian.Basic
import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Mon_

open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj

variable {C : Type*} [Category C] [CartesianMonoidalCategory C]

def IsAlbanese' (X J : C) (P : 𝟙_ C ⟶ X) [MonObj J] : Prop :=
  ∃ (ι : X ⟶ J), P ≫ ι = η[J] ∧
    ∀ {A : C} [MonObj A] (f : X ⟶ A) (_ : P ≫ f = η[A]),
      ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = ι ≫ g

namespace IsAlbanese'

variable {X P J : C} [MonObj J]

noncomputable def ofCurve' (h : IsAlbanese' X J P) : X ⟶ J := Classical.choose h

lemma comp_ofCurve' (h : IsAlbanese' X J P) : P ≫ h.ofCurve' = η[J] :=
  (Classical.choose_spec h).1

lemma exists_unique_ofCurve_comp' (h : IsAlbanese' X J P) {A : C} [MonObj A]
    (f : X ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
    ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = h.ofCurve' ≫ g :=
  (Classical.choose_spec h).2 f hf

end IsAlbanese'

structure JacWit (X : C) where
  J : C
  monObj : MonObj J
  isAlbFor : ∀ (P : 𝟙_ C ⟶ X), @IsAlbanese' C _ _ X J P monObj

variable (genusZero : C → Prop) [∀ X, Decidable (genusZero X)]

noncomputable def jacWit (X : C) : JacWit X := sorry

noncomputable def Jac (X : C) : C :=
  if genusZero X then 𝟙_ C else (jacWit genusZero X).J

variable (X : C) (P : 𝟙_ C ⟶ X)

noncomputable def ofCurveTest (P : 𝟙_ C ⟶ X) : X ⟶ Jac genusZero X := by
  unfold Jac
  split_ifs with h
  · exact toUnit X
  · letI := (jacWit genusZero X).monObj
    exact ((jacWit genusZero X).isAlbFor P).ofCurve'

-- Mock η on Jac genusZero X with instance from instance/case
instance instMonObjJac : MonObj (Jac genusZero X) := by
  unfold Jac
  split_ifs
  · infer_instance
  · exact (jacWit genusZero X).monObj

-- comp_ofCurve test
lemma compOfCurveTest : P ≫ ofCurveTest genusZero X P = η[Jac genusZero X] := by
  unfold ofCurveTest Jac
  split_ifs with h
  · apply Subsingleton.elim
  · letI := (jacWit genusZero X).monObj
    exact ((jacWit genusZero X).isAlbFor P).comp_ofCurve'

-- exists_unique_ofCurve_comp test
theorem existsUniqueTest {A : C} [MonObj A] (f : X ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
    ∃! (g : Jac genusZero X ⟶ A), f = ofCurveTest genusZero X P ≫ g := by
  unfold ofCurveTest Jac
  split_ifs with h
  · refine ⟨η[A], ?existence, ?uniqueness⟩
    case existence =>
      sorry
    case uniqueness =>
      intro g' hg'
      have hPtoUnit : P ≫ toUnit X = 𝟙 (𝟙_ C) :=
        Subsingleton.elim _ _
      have : P ≫ f = g' := by
        rw [hg', ← Category.assoc, hPtoUnit, Category.id_comp]
      rw [hf] at this
      exact this.symm
  · letI := (jacWit genusZero X).monObj
    exact ((jacWit genusZero X).isAlbFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp' f hf

8	/-!
9	# Rigidity for morphisms of group schemes (Mumford §4)
10	
11	A self-contained Phase E helper: a morphism between proper smooth geometrically
12	irreducible group schemes that agrees with another on a non-empty open subscheme
13	of the source agrees everywhere. Independent of `AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian C`
14	and provable from current Mathlib alone (Stacks 0BFD plus standard
15	properness/separatedness/irreducibility lemmas).
16	
17	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Rigidity.tex`.
18	
19	## Status (iteration 002 — refactor scaffold)
20	
21	This file is a scaffold. The body of `eq_of_eqOnOpen` is `sorry`. Subsequent
22	prover iterations are responsible for filling this sorry (Phase E of
23	`STRATEGY.md`).
24	-/
25	
26	set_option autoImplicit false
27	
28	universe u

30	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
31	
32	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
33	
34	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
35	
36	/-!
37	## Hypothesis correction (iter 003 prover)
38	
39	The original scaffold from iter 002 had hypothesis
40	`h : ∀ x ∈ U, g₁.left.base x = g₂.left.base x`
41	(point-wise topological equality on `U`). This is too weak: in characteristic
42	`p`, the absolute Frobenius `F : X → X` of an elliptic curve over `𝔽_p` agrees
43	topologically with the identity on all of `X` (the identity on the underlying
44	topological space) but `F ≠ id` as scheme morphisms — they differ on stalks
45	(`a ↦ a` vs `a ↦ a^p`). Hence the symmetric-form rigidity statement
46	`g₁.base = g₂.base on U ⇒ g₁ = g₂` is mathematically false.
47	
48	We strengthen the hypothesis to scheme-level equality on `U`:
49	`h : U.ι ≫ g₁.left = U.ι ≫ g₂.left`. This is the version that is actually
50	needed at the use site (uniqueness half of `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`):
51	the application produces *scheme-level* agreement on a non-empty open
52	(through the symmetric-power surjection), not merely topological agreement.
53	
54	We also add `[IsReduced X.left]` because Mathlib `b80f227` lacks the lemma
55	`Smooth ⇒ IsReduced` for schemes over a field (the closest is the perfect-base
56	case `Scheme.Hom.dense_smoothLocus_of_perfectField`). This is a small Mathlib
57	gap that the downstream consumer must satisfy via the smoothness + irreducibility
58	of the curve / Jacobian (e.g. via `IsIntegral`).
59	
60	The unused `[GrpObj X]`, `[GrpObj Y]`, `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n X.hom]`,
61	`[SmoothOfRelativeDimension m Y.hom]`, `[IsProper X.hom]`,
62	`[GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]` hypotheses are kept to preserve the
63	declaration's "abelian-variety" intent and forward-compatibility with the
64	informal Mumford-rigidity statement; they are now redundant with the
65	strengthened hypothesis.
66	
67	See `task_results/Rigidity.lean.md` for the full discussion.
68	-/
69	
70	/-- Rigidity for morphisms of (group) schemes (corrected, scheme-level form):
71	two morphisms `g₁, g₂ : X ⟶ Y` between schemes over `Spec k` whose restrictions
72	to a non-empty open `U ⊆ X` agree as scheme morphisms — and where `X` is
73	reduced and irreducible and `Y` is separated over `Spec k` — agree everywhere.
74	
75	The standard Mumford statement (a morphism vanishing on a non-empty open is
76	zero) is interderivable using the group law on the target. -/
77	theorem GrpObj.eq_of_eqOnOpen
78	    {n m : ℕ}
79	    {X Y : Over (Spec (.of k))}
80	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n X.hom] [IsProper X.hom]
81	    [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom] [GrpObj X]
82	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension m Y.hom] [IsProper Y.hom]
83	    [GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom] [GrpObj Y]
84	    [IsReduced X.left]
85	    (g₁ g₂ : X ⟶ Y) (U : X.left.Opens) (hU : (U : Set X.left).Nonempty)
86	    (h : (U.ι : (U : X.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ X.left) ≫ g₁.left =
87	      (U.ι : (U : X.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ X.left) ≫ g₂.left) :
88	    g₁ = g₂ := by
89	  -- (1) `Y.hom = (Y.left ↘ Spec (.of k))` is separated, since `IsProper`
90	  --     extends `IsSeparated`. We provide the instance under the
91	  --     `OverClass.fromOver` form `Y.left ↘ Spec (.of k)` so that the
92	  --     downstream typeclass search at `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'`
93	  --     finds it.
94	  haveI : IsSeparated (Y.left ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)) :=
95	    (IsProper.toIsSeparated : IsSeparated Y.hom)
96	  -- (2) `X.left` is irreducible: `Spec k` is a single point (`Unique`,
97	  --     hence `Subsingleton + Nonempty`), and `GeometricallyIrreducible`
98	  --     over a subsingleton base implies `IrreducibleSpace`.
99	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace X.left :=
100	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton X.hom
101	  -- (3) `U.ι : U.toScheme ⟶ X.left` is dominant: a non-empty open in an
102	  --     irreducible space is dense, and a dense open immersion is dominant.
103	  haveI : IsDominant (U.ι : (U : X.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ X.left) :=
104	    Scheme.PartialMap.Opens.isDominant_ι (IsOpen.dense U.isOpen hU)
105	  -- (4) Promote `g₁ = g₂` from the underlying scheme equality.
106	  --     The `Scheme.Over` and `IsOver` instances for `X.left`, `Y.left`,
107	  --     `g₁.left`, `g₂.left` over `Spec (.of k)` are inferred globally
108	  --     from the `Over (Spec (.of k))` category structure
109	  --     (`OverClass.fromOver`).
110	  refine Over.OverMorphism.ext ?_
111	  exact ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated' (S := Spec (.of k))
112	    (X := X.left) (Y := Y.left) (f := g₁.left) (g := g₂.left) U.ι h
113	
114	end AlgebraicGeometry

20	  terminal uniqueness (`Subsingleton.elim` on `Hom(𝟙_ _, 𝟙_ _)`), and the Albanese factorisation
21	  takes `g := η[A]`. Uniqueness of `g` is automatic by precomposition with `P`. *Existence*
22	  of `g` requires the classical rigidity claim: every morphism `C ⟶ A` from a genus-0 curve
23	  to an abelian variety with `P ↦ η[A]` is constant at `η[A]`. This is mathematically true
24	  (Mumford rigidity / `Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)`) but currently unavailable in Mathlib; we leave
25	  a single scoped `sorry` at the rigidity step.
26

50	to a `k`-rational point of `C`. -/

52	  unfold Jacobian
53	  split_ifs with h
54	  · -- genus C = 0 branch: Jacobian C = 𝟙_ _ (terminal). The Abel-Jacobi map is
55	    -- the unique morphism C ⟶ 𝟙_ _.

57	  · -- genus C > 0 branch: project the Albanese universal map of the chosen

61	    letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
62	    letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred

88	any morphism `f : C ⟶ A` such that `f(P) = 0` factors uniquely through the
89	Jacobian of `C`.
90	In other words, `Jacobian C` is the Albanese variety of `C`.
91	-/
92	theorem exists_unique_ofCurve_comp (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
93	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [Smooth A.hom] [IsProper A.hom] [GrpObj A]
94	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
95	    ∃! (g : Jacobian C ⟶ A), f = ofCurve P ≫ g := by
96	  unfold ofCurve Jacobian
97	  split_ifs with h
98	  · -- genus C = 0 branch.
99	    -- Goal: ∃! (g : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ A), f = toUnit C ≫ g.
100	    -- Take g := η[A]. Existence is the rigidity claim (sorry). Uniqueness follows
101	    -- by precomposition with P: from f = toUnit C ≫ g' we get
102	    --   g' = (P ≫ toUnit C) ≫ g' = P ≫ (toUnit C ≫ g') = P ≫ f = η[A],
103	    -- since P ≫ toUnit C : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ 𝟙_ _ is the identity (`Subsingleton.elim`).
104	    refine ⟨η[A], ?existence, ?uniqueness⟩
105	    case existence =>
106	      -- f = toUnit C ≫ η[A].
107	      -- Rigidity for genus-0 curves to abelian varieties: every morphism C ⟶ A
108	      -- with f ∘ P = η[A] is the constant map at η[A]. Classically true (Mumford),
109	      -- but Mathlib currently lacks the infrastructure (no `Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)`,
110	      -- no Mumford §4 rigidity theorem). Recorded as a single scoped sorry.
111	      sorry
112	    case uniqueness =>
113	      intro g' hg'

115	      --   P ≫ f = P ≫ toUnit C ≫ g' = (P ≫ toUnit C) ≫ g' = 𝟙 (𝟙_ _) ≫ g' = g'
116	      -- (using `Subsingleton.elim` on the terminal hom-set).
117	      have hPtoUnit : P ≫ toUnit C = 𝟙 (𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))) :=
118	        Subsingleton.elim _ _

120	        rw [hg', ← Category.assoc, hPtoUnit, Category.id_comp]
121	      -- Combine with hf : P ≫ f = η[A]:
122	      rw [hf] at this
123	      exact this.symm
124	  · -- genus C > 0 branch: reduce to the witness's
125	    -- `IsAlbanese.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`.
126	    letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
127	    letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper

130	    exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf

import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Cartesian.Basic
import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Mon_

open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj

variable {C : Type*} [Category C] [CartesianMonoidalCategory C]

def IsAlbanese' (X J : C) (P : 𝟙_ C ⟶ X) [MonObj J] : Prop :=
  ∃ (ι : X ⟶ J), P ≫ ι = η[J] ∧
    ∀ {A : C} [MonObj A] (f : X ⟶ A) (_ : P ≫ f = η[A]),
      ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = ι ≫ g

namespace IsAlbanese'

variable {X P J : C} [MonObj J]

noncomputable def ofCurve' (h : IsAlbanese' X J P) : X ⟶ J := Classical.choose h

lemma comp_ofCurve' (h : IsAlbanese' X J P) : P ≫ h.ofCurve' = η[J] :=
  (Classical.choose_spec h).1

lemma exists_unique_ofCurve_comp' (h : IsAlbanese' X J P) {A : C} [MonObj A]
    (f : X ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
    ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = h.ofCurve' ≫ g :=
  (Classical.choose_spec h).2 f hf

end IsAlbanese'

structure JacWit (X : C) where
  J : C
  monObj : MonObj J
  isAlbFor : ∀ (P : 𝟙_ C ⟶ X), @IsAlbanese' C _ _ X J P monObj

variable (genusZero : C → Prop) [∀ X, Decidable (genusZero X)]
  (jacWit : ∀ X, JacWit X)

noncomputable def Jac (X : C) : C :=
  if genusZero X then 𝟙_ C else (jacWit X).J

variable (X : C) (P : 𝟙_ C ⟶ X)

noncomputable def ofCurveTest (P : 𝟙_ C ⟶ X) : X ⟶ Jac genusZero jacWit X := by
  unfold Jac
  split_ifs with h
  · exact toUnit X
  · letI := (jacWit X).monObj
    exact ((jacWit X).isAlbFor P).ofCurve'

instance instMonObjJac : MonObj (Jac genusZero jacWit X) := by
  unfold Jac
  split_ifs
  · infer_instance
  · exact (jacWit X).monObj

lemma compOfCurveTest : P ≫ ofCurveTest genusZero jacWit X P = η[Jac genusZero jacWit X] := by
  unfold ofCurveTest Jac
  split_ifs with h
  · apply Subsingleton.elim
  · letI := (jacWit X).monObj
    exact ((jacWit X).isAlbFor P).comp_ofCurve'

theorem existsUniqueTest {A : C} [MonObj A] (f : X ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
    ∃! (g : Jac genusZero jacWit X ⟶ A), f = ofCurveTest genusZero jacWit X P ≫ g := by
  unfold ofCurveTest Jac
  split_ifs with h
  · refine ⟨η[A], ?existence, ?uniqueness⟩
    case existence =>
      sorry
    case uniqueness =>
      intro g' hg'
      have hPtoUnit : P ≫ toUnit X = 𝟙 (𝟙_ C) := Subsingleton.elim _ _
      have : P ≫ f = g' := by
        rw [hg', ← Category.assoc, hPtoUnit, Category.id_comp]
      rw [hf] at this
      exact this.symm
  · letI := (jacWit X).monObj
    exact ((jacWit X).isAlbFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp' f hf

#print axioms compOfCurveTest
#print axioms existsUniqueTest

# AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean

## Iteration 072 — Lane 2 (Phase E unblocked)

**File trajectory**: 3 sorries → 1 sorry (rigidity-blocked, mathematically essential).

**Strategy**: case-split each protected declaration on `genus C = 0`, navigating the
`dite` in `Jacobian C` via `unfold Jacobian; split_ifs with h`. In the `genus C > 0`
branch, project to the post-refactor witness's `isAlbaneseFor P` Albanese predicate.
In the `genus C = 0` branch, work directly with the terminal object.

**Critical precondition**: written against the **target post-refactor shape** of
`JacobianWitness` (per PROGRESS.md Iter-072 Lane 1):

- `JacobianWitness C` has fields `J`, `grpObj`, `proper`, `smooth`, `geomIrred`,
  `smoothGenus`, `isAlbaneseFor : ∀ (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C), IsAlbanese C P J` (no `P` field).
- `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P : IsAlbanese C P (jacobianWitness C).J`.

If Lane 1's refactor lands as specified, this file compiles. If Lane 1 ships a
different shape (e.g. `jacobianWitness C P` with `P` as parameter rather than
`isAlbaneseFor P` on a `P`-free witness), the field references need a one-line
rename. The architectural intent (case-split + Albanese projection) is shape-stable.

## ofCurve (L51)
### Attempt 1
- **Approach**: `unfold Jacobian; split_ifs with h`. In genus-0 branch, return
  `toUnit C : C ⟶ 𝟙_ _`. In genus>0 branch, `letI` the four
  `(jacobianWitness C).{grpObj, proper, smooth, geomIrred}` fields as local
  instances so that `IsAlbanese.ofCurve`'s implicit `[GrpObj J]` etc. unify, then
  `exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve`.
- **Marker**: declaration was `def`; promoted to `noncomputable def` (allowed under
  the 2026-05-07 user authorisation; the genus>0 branch goes through `Classical.choose`
  on the `IsAlbanese` existential).
- **Result**: RESOLVED.
- **Pattern verified**: tested via `lean_run_code` against a CartesianMonoidal mock
  (full mock — `IsAlbanese'`, `JacWit`, `Jac` as `dite`, `ofCurveTest`,
  `compOfCurveTest`, `existsUniqueTest` — compiled clean with the one expected
  rigidity sorry).

## comp_ofCurve (L68)
### Attempt 1
- **Approach**: `unfold ofCurve Jacobian; split_ifs with h`. Genus-0 branch:
  both sides live in `Hom(𝟙_ _, 𝟙_ _)` which is `Unique` (hence `Subsingleton`),
  so `apply Subsingleton.elim` closes. Genus>0 branch: `letI` the witness instances
  and `exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).comp_ofCurve`.
- **Result**: RESOLVED.
- **Key Mathlib facts used**:
  - `CartesianMonoidalCategory.Basic.lean:70`: `instance (X : C) : Unique (X ⟶ 𝟙_ C)`,
    giving `Subsingleton (𝟙_ _ ⟶ 𝟙_ _)`.
  - `Mon_.lean:132–137`: standard `MonObj (𝟙_ C)` instance with `one := 𝟙 _`,
    so `η[𝟙_ _] = 𝟙 (𝟙_ _)` reduces by `rfl`.

## exists_unique_ofCurve_comp (L92)
### Attempt 1 — genus > 0 branch
- **Approach**: same `unfold ofCurve Jacobian; split_ifs with h`. In the
  `genus C > 0` branch, `letI` the witness's four instance fields and apply
  `((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf`
  (the iter-069 extractor on `IsAlbanese`, defined at `Jacobian.lean:70-75`).
- **Result**: RESOLVED (genus>0 branch fully closed).

### Attempt 1 — genus = 0 branch (partial)
- **Approach**: `refine ⟨η[A], ?existence, ?uniqueness⟩`.
  - **Uniqueness** (RESOLVED): given `hg' : f = toUnit C ≫ g'`, precompose with
    `P`: `P ≫ f = P ≫ toUnit C ≫ g' = 𝟙 (𝟙_ _) ≫ g' = g'` (using
    `Subsingleton.elim` on `P ≫ toUnit C : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ 𝟙_ _`). Combined with
    `hf : P ≫ f = η[A]`, we get `g' = η[A]`. Proof inline at L113–123.
  - **Existence** (SORRY): need `f = toUnit C ≫ η[A]`. This is the classical
    rigidity claim: every morphism `f : C ⟶ A` from a smooth proper geometrically
    irreducible genus-0 curve to an abelian variety with `f ∘ P = η[A]` is constant
    at `η[A]`. Equivalently, `Hom(C, A) = A(k)` as soon as `genus C = 0`.

### Why the genus-0 existence step needs a sorry

The genus-0 existence step is **mathematically essential rigidity**, not a missing
Mathlib lemma in the usual sense. Specifically:

- Over an algebraically closed field, genus-0 smooth proper geometrically
  irreducible curves are `ℙ¹`, and `Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)` for `A` an abelian variety
  (the standard argument: `Pic⁰(ℙ¹) = 0`, so any morphism `ℙ¹ → A` is a translate
  of a trivial-line-bundle morphism, hence constant).
- Over a general field `k`, genus-0 curves with a `k`-rational point are still
  isomorphic to `ℙ¹_k`, but the proof requires Brauer–Severi machinery. The
  conclusion `Hom(C, A) = A(k)` still holds.
- Neither route is currently formalised in Mathlib (no `Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)` lemma,
  no general Mumford §4 rigidity for morphisms to abelian varieties). The local
  helper `GrpObj.eq_of_eqOnOpen` in `AlgebraicJacobian/Rigidity.lean` proves a
  *related* but weaker rigidity (two morphisms agreeing on a dense open agree
  everywhere); it does not yield "morphism from genus-0 curve is constant".

The rigidity claim is **not** specific to the Albanese framework — it is the
intrinsic content of "the Jacobian of a genus-0 curve is the trivial group
scheme". Any honest proof of `IsAlbanese C P (𝟙_ _)` for genus-0 `C` requires
exactly this rigidity input.

### Possible routes for the plan agent (post-iter-072)

1. **Direct rigidity helper** in `Rigidity.lean`:
   ```
   theorem const_of_genusZero {C A : Over (Spec (.of k))}
       [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
       [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] (hg : genus C = 0)
       [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
       (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C) (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
       f = toUnit C ≫ η[A]
   ```
   This is a substantial new theorem; closure depends on building enough
   infrastructure for the `Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)` claim (likely requires
   `Pic⁰(C) = 0 ⇔ genus C = 0` plus a "constant morphism" characterisation
   via dualisation).

2. **Witness existence with `J := 𝟙_ _` in genus 0**: have `nonempty_jacobianWitness`
   return a genus-0-specific witness with `J := 𝟙_ _` and `isAlbaneseFor`
   *itself* discharging the rigidity claim. The sorry then migrates from
   `AbelJacobi.lean` to `Jacobian.lean`'s existence theorem, but the
   mathematical content is identical.

3. **Defer the genus-0 case**: package the genus-0 obligation into a separate
   protected hypothesis (e.g. `RigidityForGenusZero C`) and have
   `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`'s genus-0 branch project from that hypothesis.
   Mathematically this is option (1) under a different name; structurally it
   isolates the sorry to a single new declaration. Probably not worth the
   ceremony.

### Estimated effort for option (1) honest closure

Building `Hom(ℙ¹_k, A) = A(k)` from scratch in Mathlib is a multi-week effort:
- Need a workable `IsBrauerSeveri C ↔ genus C = 0` characterisation (or skip via
  geometric base change).
- Need `Pic⁰(ℙ¹_k) = 0` (this is closer to Mathlib's reach via
  `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum`).
- Need a `morphism-to-abelian-variety classifies via Pic⁰` adjunction.
- Each step is a substantial theorem in its own right.

The honest closure of the genus-0 rigidity sorry is therefore **strictly outside
the scope of iter-072** and likely outside the scope of the project's near-term
roadmap. It is on par with `nonempty_jacobianWitness` (the deferred Phase C
existence claim) as a major mathematical obligation.

## Verification

- Self-contained pattern test via `lean_run_code` (full mock IsAlbanese / JacWit /
  Jac / ofCurveTest / compOfCurveTest / existsUniqueTest): **PASS** (only the
  rigidity sorry remains, as expected).
- Project-level `lake env lean` / `lean_diagnostic_messages`: **unavailable**
  (build env broken since iter-069 per PROGRESS.md). The deterministic
  `sync_leanok` phase (post-prover, pre-review) will run the authoritative check.
- `Jacobian.lean` reference (Lane 1): I depend on Lane 1's refactor renaming
  `isAlbanese` → `isAlbaneseFor` and dropping the `P` field. If Lane 1's lane
  lands with a different name, the three `letI ... exact (...isAlbaneseFor P)...`
  uses need a one-token rewrite.

## Sorry inventory (post-iter-072)

| Line | Decl | Status | Mathematical content |
|---|---|---|---|
| L111 (case existence) | `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` | **SORRY** | Genus-0 rigidity: `Hom(genus-0 curve, abelian variety) = constants`. Mumford §4 / `Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)` style. |

**Net delta**: 3 → 1 (−2).

## Blueprint readiness

- `def:ofCurve` (`Jacobian.ofCurve`) — formal body now present; ready for `\leanok`
  in chapter statement block (deterministic `sync_leanok` will handle).
- `lem:comp_ofCurve` (`Jacobian.comp_ofCurve`) — fully closed; ready for `\leanok`
  on both statement *and* proof blocks.
- `thm:exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` (`Jacobian.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`) —
  statement formalised, proof closed except for one rigidity sorry. The
  declaration has a `sorry`, so the proof block stays unmarked; the statement
  block is `\leanok`-eligible. `sync_leanok` will determine.

The blueprint chapter `AbelJacobi.tex` already aligns with this implementation
(Albanese-framework route, deferred existence acknowledged); no edits requested
from the plan agent.

## Plan agent notes

- Iter-072 Lane 2 reduces this file from 3 sorries to 1. The remaining sorry
  is a **substantive mathematical obligation** (genus-0 rigidity), not a
  Mathlib-name lookup failure. Estimated effort for honest closure: weeks
  (Brauer–Severi or `Pic⁰(ℙ¹) = 0` infrastructure).
- The architectural decomposition (`isAlbaneseFor P` extractor + case-split)
  is robust and ready to consume Lane 1's refactor.
- No new axioms introduced.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.
