1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	
5	prover (iteration 072 — four parallel lanes).
6	
7	## Stages
8	- [x] init
9	- [x] autoformalize
10	- [ ] prover
11	- [ ] polish
12	
13	## Big-picture context
14	
15	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
16	
17	`STRATEGY.md` describes the five-phase build-out plan. After iter-071's three-track prover round (−7 net sorries, the largest single-iter delta since iter-064), the sorry landscape is:
18	
19	| File | Sorries | Phase | Status |
20	|---|---|---|---|
21	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` | **6** | A | iter-072 Lane 4 |
22	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` | **8** | B | iter-072 Lane 3 |
23	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` | **1** | C/D (consolidated) | iter-072 Lane 1 (refactor) |
24	| `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` | 3 | E (now unblocked) | iter-072 Lane 2 |
25	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` | 1 | C-rep (deferred) | off-limits |
26	| **Total** | **19** | | |
27	
28	Iter-071's structural highlight was the `JacobianWitness` single-witness consolidation pattern on `Jacobian.lean`, which collapsed five originally-protected sorries into one unprotected existence hypothesis. This unblocks Phase E at the type-checking level — `Jacobian C` is now well-typed for both genus branches, so `AbelJacobi.lean`'s three sorries are ready for closure.
29	
30	## Current Objectives
31	
32	The dispatcher fans out one prover per file listed below. Each objective is self-contained — the prover does **not** see the strategy file.
33	
34	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` — Phase C consolidation refactor (precursor to Lane 2)
35	
36	**File status.** 1 syntactic sorry, 228 LOC. Theorem under attack: `nonempty_jacobianWitness` (L162). Iter-071 prover bundled the five originally-protected sorries (`Jacobian` g>0 branch, `instGrpObj`, `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`, `instGeometricallyIrreducible`) into a single existence hypothesis via the `JacobianWitness` structure, with the four protected instances projecting the relevant field of `(jacobianWitness C)`.
37	
38	**Iter-072 target.** **Refactor — do not attempt to close the existence sorry**. The `JacobianWitness` structure currently fixes an internal marked point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C` as a field, with `isAlbanese : @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred` indexed on that internal `P`. This is incompatible with `AbelJacobi.Jacobian.ofCurve P` (whose `P` is an arbitrary input). Refactor as follows:
39	
40	1. **Remove the `P` field from `JacobianWitness`.** The bundle should no longer carry a marked point.
41	2. **Parameterise the `isAlbanese` field on `P`**:
42	   ```
43	   isAlbaneseFor : ∀ (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C), @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred
44	   ```
45	   so that the same Albanese object `J` serves as Albanese for every choice of pointing. This is mathematically correct: the *scheme* `J` is intrinsic; only the universal morphism depends on `P`.
46	3. **`nonempty_jacobianWitness` is unchanged in signature** (`Nonempty (JacobianWitness C) := sorry`), since `P` is no longer a parameter of the bundle.
47	4. **`jacobianWitness C` unchanged** (`Classical.choice (nonempty_jacobianWitness C)`).
48	5. **The four protected instances are unchanged in proof** — each still projects the relevant field of `jacobianWitness C` (which no longer has a `P` field but still has `grpObj`, `proper`, `smooth`, `geomIrred`, `smoothGenus`, `J`).
49	6. **`Jacobian C` unchanged** — its g>0 branch still projects `(jacobianWitness C).J`.
50	
51	Verify the single remaining sorry is `nonempty_jacobianWitness` and that all four protected instances still discharge. Document the new shape in your task result so Lane 2 can rely on it.
52	
53	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Jacobian.tex` (§ *The Albanese universal property*, *The Jacobian definition*, *Existence of an Albanese variety*).
54	
55	**Hard constraints.** Protected signatures (`Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`, `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`, `instGeometricallyIrreducible`) frozen. No new axioms. **Do not attempt `nonempty_jacobianWitness`** — it is the deferred Phase C existence claim (no symmetric powers, no FGA representability in current Mathlib).
56	
57	### 2. `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` — Phase E unblocked
58	
59	**File status.** 3 syntactic sorries, 57 LOC. Three protected declarations, all bare `:= sorry` / `by sorry`:
60	
61	| Decl | Line | Type |
62	|---|---|---|
63	| `Jacobian.ofCurve` | L34 | `(P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C) → C ⟶ Jacobian C` |
64	| `Jacobian.comp_ofCurve` | L39 | `P ≫ ofCurve P = η[Jacobian C]` |
65	| `Jacobian.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` | L49 | Albanese factorisation existence + uniqueness |
66	
67	**Iter-072 target.** Close all three sorries via case-split on `genus C = 0` + witness projection. **Precondition**: this iteration's Lane 1 (`Jacobian.lean` prover) is concurrently refactoring `JacobianWitness` to parameterise `isAlbanese` on `P`. Write your code against the **target post-refactor shape**:
68	
69	- `JacobianWitness C` has no `P` field.
70	- `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P : IsAlbanese C P (jacobianWitness C).J` — a forall-indexed Albanese predicate.
71	- The four protected instances on `Jacobian C` are independent of the choice of `P`.
72	
73	If Lane 1's refactor has not yet landed when your edits compile, your edits will reference field/method names that do not exist; the compile error will be informative and iter-073 will resolve. The architectural decision (parameterised `isAlbaneseFor`) is fixed.
74	
75	**Strategy per declaration.**
76	
77	- **`ofCurve P`** (L34). Two cases:
78	  - `genus C = 0`: `Jacobian C = 𝟙_ _` (terminal). The unique morphism `C ⟶ 𝟙_ _` works; for `Over (Spec (.of k))` this is `toUnit C` (the unique map to the terminal object).
79	  - `genus C ≠ 0`: `Jacobian C = (jacobianWitness C).J`. Use `((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve : C ⟶ (jacobianWitness C).J`.
80	  - Recall `noncomputable def ofCurve` is allowed under the 2026-05-07 user authorisation.
81	
82	- **`comp_ofCurve P`** (L39). Two cases:
83	  - `genus C = 0`: every morphism into the terminal object is unique, so `P ≫ ofCurve P = η[Jacobian C]` by uniqueness. Use `Unique.eq_default` or `Subsingleton.elim` on `Hom (𝟙_ _) (𝟙_ _)` (one-element, identity).
84	  - `genus C ≠ 0`: `((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).comp_ofCurve : P ≫ ((isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve) = η[(jacobianWitness C).J]`.
85	
86	- **`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp P f hf`** (L49). Albanese factorisation:
87	  - `genus C = 0`: any `g : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ A` is unique (the unique map *out* of the terminal object is constrained iff `A` has a unique map *from* `𝟙_ _`, which holds when `A` has a global section). Actually, for `A` with `[GrpObj A]`, the neutral element `η[A] : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ A` is the canonical choice; uniqueness follows from `hf : P ≫ f = η[A]` and the genus-0 collapse making `f = η[A] ∘ (terminal map)`. Concretely: `g := η[A]`, `hg_eq : f = ofCurve P ≫ g` by computing both sides on the terminal object.
88	  - `genus C ≠ 0`: `((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf` (using the iter-069 `IsAlbanese.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` extractor).
89	
90	The `IsAlbanese` namespace already contains the three extractors (`ofCurve`, `comp_ofCurve`, `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`) — see `Jacobian.lean` L61-75.
91	
92	**Mathlib lookups likely needed:**
93	- For the terminal-object branch: `CartesianMonoidalCategory.toUnit`, `Limits.IsTerminal.uniqueUpToIso`, `Subsingleton.elim` on terminal-object Hom-sets.
94	- For `[GrpObj A]` and `η[A]`: `MonObj.one_def`, `η[]` notation defined in `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group`.
95	
96	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/AbelJacobi.tex`.
97

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Search Protocol
66	
67	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
68	
69	1. `lean_local_search` first
70	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
71	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
72	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
73	
74	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
75	
76	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
77	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
78	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
79	
80	## Logging
81	
82	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
83	
84	**Format:**
85	
86	```markdown
87	# Algebra/WLocal.lean

92	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
93	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing

95	### Attempt 2

97	- **Result:** RESOLVED
98	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
99	
100	## helper_bijective (line 78)
101	### Attempt 1
102	- **Approach:** Function.Bijective.comp
103	- **Result:** IN PROGRESS — stuck on surjectivity
104	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective
105	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
106	```
107	
108	**Rules:**
109	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
110	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
111	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
112	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
113	
114	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
115	
116	## Summary Pipeline
117	
118	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
119	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
120	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
121	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
122	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
123	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
124	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks

16	/-! # Relative Kähler differentials for schemes
17	
18	This file constructs the sheaf of relative Kähler differentials `Ω_{X/S}`
19	of a morphism of schemes `f : X → S`, together with its universal derivation
20	`d : 𝒪_X → Ω_{X/S}`. It also states the cotangent exact sequence and the
21	characterisation of smoothness in terms of local freeness of `Ω`.
22	
23	The construction builds on Mathlib's ring-theoretic `KaehlerDifferential`
24	and the presheaf-of-modules differential construction
25	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`.
26	
27	## Status (iteration 064 — scaffold)
28	
29	All main declarations have `sorry` bodies. Closure trajectory is estimated
30	at ~10 iterations per `STRATEGY.md`.
31	
32	## References
33	
34	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
35	-/
36	
37	set_option autoImplicit false
38	
39	universe u
40	
41	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
42	
43	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
44	
45	variable {X Y S : Scheme.{u}}
46	
47	/-! ## The relative cotangent presheaf and sheaf -/
48	
49	/-- The relative cotangent **presheaf** of a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ S`.
50	
51	On each open `U ⊆ X`, the sections are the Kähler differential module of
52	the ring map `O_S(f(U)) → O_X(U)` induced by `f`. More precisely, we use
53	the inverse-image presheaf `f⁻¹ O_S` on `X` and the canonical map to `O_X`;
54	the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.
55	
56	Mathlib leverage: `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` for `f⁻¹`,
57	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
58	for the presheaf of Kähler differentials. -/
59	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
60	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
61	    f.c
62	  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'
63	
64	/-! ### Sheaf condition: decomposition
65	
66	Closing `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` is the main outstanding
67	gap of Phase B step 1. The mathematical proof has three layers:
68	
69	1. **`KaehlerDifferential` commutes with localisation.** For a ring map
70	   `A → B` and `f ∈ B`, `Ω_{B[1/f]/A} ≅ Ω_{B/A} ⊗_B B[1/f]`. Mathlib's
71	   `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule` (and the scheme variant
72	   `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`) provides this.
73	2. **The presheaf is a sheaf on the basis of affine basic opens.** On a
74	   basic open `D(f)` of `Spec B`, sections are `Ω_{B[1/f]/A}`. Gluing on a
75	   basic-open cover `{D(fᵢ)}` of `Spec B` follows from the localisation
76	   compatibility of step 1, applied to each `fᵢ`.
77	3. **Globalisation to all opens.** A presheaf on a scheme is a sheaf iff
78	   it is a sheaf on the basis of affine opens (more precisely, on the
79	   basic opens of every affine open). This is the standard scheme
80	   sheaf-of-modules construction.
81	
82	The substep theorems below are the natural decomposition. Each remains
83	`sorry` pending the full proof; the structure mirrors the blueprint at
84	`blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`. -/
85	
86	/-- Substep 1 (localisation compatibility): For an affine open `V` of `X`
87	mapped to an affine open `U` of `S`, the sections of
88	`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf` over `V` are isomorphic (as additive groups)
89	to the Kähler differential module `KaehlerDifferential (φ'.app (op V))`. -/
90	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler (f : X ⟶ S)
91	    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
92	    ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj V : Type _) =
93	      CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential
94	        (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat
95	          f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c |>.app V) :=
96	  rfl
97	
98	/-- The presheaf of relative differentials is a **sheaf** in the Zariski
99	topology: Kähler differentials commute with localisation, which gives the
100	gluing axiom on affine opens.
101	
102	**Status (iter-065):** Sorry. Proof requires combining:
103	- Substep 1 (`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler`, definitional)
104	- Substep 2: sheaf condition on the basis of basic opens, derived from
105	  `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule`
106	- Substep 3: globalisation from a basis to all opens (Mathlib has
107	  `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.isSheafUniqueGluing` and `isSheafFor` lemmas
108	  for restricting to a basis)
109	
110	The route via `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` is not viable since that
111	requires the result we are trying to prove (the presheaf must already be
112	a sheaf to be packaged as a `SheafOfModules`). -/
113	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf (f : X ⟶ S) :
114	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat)
115	      (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf := by
116	  -- Strategy: reduce to the underlying presheaf of types via
117	  -- `isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp`, then verify the sheaf condition on
118	  -- affine opens using `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`.
119	  -- The proof requires substantial development of a
120	  -- "sheaf condition restricted to affine basis" lemma + the localisation
121	  -- compatibility for Kähler differentials in the scheme-language.
122	  sorry
123	
124	/-- The sheaf of relative differentials `Ω_{X/S}`.
125	
126	Packages the presheaf `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf` with the sheaf axiom
127	`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf` into the standard `X.Modules` shape. -/
128	noncomputable def relativeDifferentials (f : X ⟶ S) : X.Modules :=
129	  ⟨relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f, relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf f⟩
130	
131	/-! ## Universal derivation -/
132	
133	/-- The universal derivation `d : 𝒪_X → Ω_{X/S}`.
134	
135	On each affine open `V = Spec B` over `Spec A`, this is the Mathlib universal
136	derivation `d_{B/A} : B → Ω_{B/A}`. It is an `A`-linear sheaf map satisfying
137	the Leibniz rule `d(ab) = a d(b) + b d(a)`. -/
138	noncomputable def universalDerivation (f : X ⟶ S) :
139	    X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat ⟶
140	      (relativeDifferentials f).val.presheaf := by
141	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
142	    f.c
143	  let d' := PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ'
144	  have h_eq : X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat := rfl
145	  refine {
146	    app := fun U ↦ AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (d'.d (X := U)),
147	    naturality := fun U V g ↦ ?naturality
148	  }
149	  case naturality =>
150	    ext x
151	    simp only [sheafCompose_obj_obj, PresheafOfModules.presheaf_obj_coe, Functor.comp_obj,
152	      CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj, Functor.comp_map, AddCommGrpCat.hom_comp]
153	    suffices d'.d ((ConcreteCategory.hom (X.presheaf.map g)) x) =
154	        (ConcreteCategory.hom ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).map g)) (d'.d x) by
155	      simpa using this
156	    exact d'.d_map g x
157	
158	/-! ## Cotangent exact sequence
159	
160	The cotangent exact sequence
161	```
162	  f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y} ⟶ 0
163	```
164	for a composition `X ⟶ Y ⟶ S` is decomposed into five named sub-claims:
165	
166	1. `cotangentExactSeqAlpha` — the base-change cotangent map
167	   `f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S}`. Locally, this is
168	   `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange A A' B`, where `A → A' → B` is the
169	   tower of structure ring maps on an affine chart.
170	2. `cotangentExactSeqBeta` — the relative-quotient cotangent map
171	   `Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y}`. Locally, this is the surjection
172	   `KaehlerDifferential.map A A' B B` induced by the inclusion `A → A'`
173	   that kills the `A`-linear derivations factoring through `A'`.
174	3. `cotangentExactSeqAlpha_comp_Beta` — composition zero `α ≫ β = 0`,
175	   from the ring-level `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` (the
176	   range of `mapBaseChange` lies in the kernel of `map`).
177	4. `cotangentExactSeqShortComplexExact` — exactness `ker β = im α`,
178	   from the same ring-level theorem and gluing.
179	5. `cotangentExactSeqBetaEpi` — `β` is an epi, from
180	   `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective` applied on each affine chart.
181	
182	The headline theorem `cotangent_exact_sequence` then assembles the
183	existential bundle from these five components without further sorries. -/
184	
185	/-- The base-change cotangent map `f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S}`.
186	
187	For a composition `X ⟶ Y ⟶ S`, this is the natural map whose local
188	description on an affine chart with rings `A = O_S(W)`, `A' = O_Y(f(V))`,
189	`B = O_X(V)` is `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange A A' B`:
190	```
191	  Ω_{A'/A} ⊗_{A'} B ⟶ Ω_{B/A}
192	```
193	sending `da ⊗ b ↦ b · da` (where the right-hand `da` is the universal
194	derivation in `Ω_{B/A}`).
195	
196	**Status:** sorry — the global construction requires gluing the local
197	base-change maps, mediated by `PresheafOfModules.homMk` applied to the
198	ring-level `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange`. -/
199	noncomputable def cotangentExactSeqAlpha (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
200	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (relativeDifferentials g) ⟶
201	      relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g) := by
202	  -- Strategy. Apply the `pullback ⊣ pushforward` adjunction to convert the goal into the
203	  -- adjoint hom on `Y`, then build that hom from its underlying presheaf-of-modules morphism via
204	  -- the universal property of `relativeDifferentials' φ_g'`.
205	  --
206	  -- After `homEquiv.symm`, the goal becomes
207	  --   `relativeDifferentials g ⟶ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj (relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g))`,
208	  -- both `Y.Modules`. Their underlying presheaves are
209	  --   LHS.val = relativeDifferentialsPresheaf g
210	  --           = PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ_g'
211	  --   RHS.val = (PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj
212	  --               (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g))
213	  -- so it suffices to build a `PresheafOfModules` morphism between the underlying values, then
214	  -- package via `⟨·⟩` as a `SheafOfModules.Hom`.
215	  refine ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _).symm ?_
216	  -- Set up the two ring presheaf morphisms that drive the relativeDifferentials' construction.
217	  let φ_g' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat g.base).homEquiv
218	    S.presheaf Y.presheaf).symm g.c
219	  let φ_fg' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).homEquiv
220	    S.presheaf X.presheaf).symm (f ≫ g).c
221	  -- The presheaf-level morphism is the descent of the universal derivation of
222	  -- `relativeDifferentials' φ_g'` to the target, where the target carries the natural
223	  -- `Derivation' φ_g'` structure obtained by precomposing the universal derivation
224	  -- `D_{(f ≫ g)/S}` with the ring presheaf morphism `f.c.app U : Y.presheaf(U) → X.presheaf(f⁻¹U)`.
225	  --
226	  -- That is: define `d_target.d (U) (b) := D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom b)` where
227	  -- `D_X = PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_fg'`.
228	  -- The Leibniz (`d_mul`) and naturality (`d_map`) axioms follow respectively from the
229	  -- ring-hom property of `f.c.app U` and the naturality of `f.c`, together with the
230	  -- derivation laws on `D_X`.
231	  -- The vanishing condition `d (φ_g'.app U a) = 0` follows from the adjunction-coherence
232	  -- identity `(f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom.app U) ∘ (φ_g'.app U)
233	  --   = (φ_fg'.app (f⁻¹U)) ∘ (pullback-naturality map)`
234	  -- (an instance of the `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` naturality across the composition
235	  -- `f ≫ g`), combined with `D_X.d_app`.
236	  -- Substep (i): produce the morphism of presheaves of Y-modules.
237	  -- This is the substantive sorry; the decomposition above splits naturally into four named
238	  -- subgoals (`d`, `d_mul`, `d_map`, `d_app`) once the `Derivation'` constructor is unfolded.
239	  let presheafHom : relativeDifferentialsPresheaf g ⟶
240	      (PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj
241	        (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g)) :=
242	    (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').desc sorry
243	  exact ⟨presheafHom⟩
244	
245	/-- The relative-quotient cotangent map `Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y}`.
246	
247	For a composition `X ⟶ Y ⟶ S`, this is the natural surjection whose
248	local description on an affine chart with rings `A = O_S(W)`,
249	`A' = O_Y(f(V))`, `B = O_X(V)` is `KaehlerDifferential.map A A' B B`:
250	```
251	  Ω_{B/A} ⟶ Ω_{B/A'}
252	```
253	sending the universal `A`-linear derivation to the universal `A'`-linear
254	derivation.
255	
256	**Status:** sorry — the global construction follows from the morphism of
257	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
258	induced by the natural transformation `(f ≫ g)⁻¹ O_S ⟶ f⁻¹ O_Y` between
259	the pullback ring presheaves. -/
260	noncomputable def cotangentExactSeqBeta (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
261	    relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g) ⟶ relativeDifferentials f := by
262	  let φ1' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).homEquiv
263	    S.presheaf X.presheaf).symm (f ≫ g).c
264	  let φ2' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv
265	    Y.presheaf X.presheaf).symm f.c
266	  let adj_f := TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base
267	  let adj_fg := TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base
268	  let η := ((adj_fg.homEquiv S.presheaf ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj
269	    Y.presheaf)).symm (g.c ≫ (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map
270	      (adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf)))
271	  have hη : η ≫ φ2' = φ1' := by
272	    have h1 : adj_fg.homEquiv S.presheaf X.presheaf (η ≫ φ2') =
273	        adj_fg.homEquiv S.presheaf ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj Y.presheaf) η ≫
274	          (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).map φ2' := by
275	      rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right]
276	    have h2 : adj_fg.homEquiv S.presheaf ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj Y.presheaf) η =
277	        g.c ≫ (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map (adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf) := by
278	      dsimp [η]
279	      exact Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _
280	    have h3 : (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).map φ2' =
281	        (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map
282	          ((TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ2') := rfl
283	    have h4 : adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫ (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ2' = f.c := by
284	      have : adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf ≫ (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ2' =
285	          adj_f.homEquiv Y.presheaf X.presheaf φ2' := rfl
286	      rw [this]
287	      exact Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _
288	    have h5 : adj_fg.homEquiv S.presheaf X.presheaf (η ≫ φ2') = (f ≫ g).c := by
289	      rw [h1, h2, h3]
290	      have h6 : (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map (adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf) ≫
291	          (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map
292	            ((TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ2') =
293	          (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map f.c := by
294	        rw [← Functor.map_comp]
295	        congr 1
296	        exact h4
297	      calc
298	        (g.c ≫ (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map (adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf)) ≫
299	            (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map
300	              ((TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ2') =
301	          g.c ≫ ((TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map (adj_f.unit.app Y.presheaf) ≫
302	            (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map
303	              ((TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ2')) := by
304	          simp only [Category.assoc]
305	        _ = g.c ≫ (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat g.base).map f.c := by erw [h6]
306	        _ = (f ≫ g).c := by exact (LocallyRingedSpace.comp_c f g).symm
307	    have h7 : adj_fg.homEquiv S.presheaf X.presheaf φ1' = (f ≫ g).c := by
308	      dsimp [φ1']
309	      exact Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _
310	    rw [← h7] at h5
311	    exact (adj_fg.homEquiv S.presheaf X.presheaf).injective h5
312	  let d2 := PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ2'
313	  let d1 : (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ2').Derivation' φ1' := {
314	    d := d2.d
315	    d_mul := d2.d_mul
316	    d_map := d2.d_map
317	    d_app := fun {X} a => by
318	      have h : φ1'.app X a = φ2'.app X (η.app X a) := by
319	        rw [← hη]
320	        rfl
321	      rw [h]
322	      exact d2.d_app (η.app X a)
323	  }
324	  let presheafHom := (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ1').desc d1
325	  exact ⟨presheafHom⟩

327	/-- The composition `α ≫ β = 0` of the two cotangent maps.
328	
329	On each affine chart `A → A' → B`, this is the statement that
330	`KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange A A' B ≫ KaehlerDifferential.map A A' B B = 0`,
331	which follows directly from `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map`
332	(the range of the first map is contained in the kernel of the second).
333	
334	**Status:** sorry — bundles the three remaining claims (composition zero,
335	exactness of the short complex, and `β` being an epi). All three are
336	local properties whose ring-level inputs are:
337	- composition zero: `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` (the
338	  range of `mapBaseChange` lies in the kernel of `map`)
339	- exactness: same theorem (the range equals the kernel)
340	- epi from `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective R S B`
341	
342	A future iteration may split this bundled theorem into three named
343	sub-claims once the ingredients land. -/
344	lemma cotangentExactSeq_structure (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
345	    ∃ (h : cotangentExactSeqAlpha f g ≫ cotangentExactSeqBeta f g = 0),
346	      (CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.mk
347	        (cotangentExactSeqAlpha f g) (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g) h).Exact ∧
348	      CategoryTheory.Epi (cotangentExactSeqBeta f g) := by
349	  -- Decomposition: produce the three named sub-claims in order. Each has a distinct
350	  -- ring-theoretic input via `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` / `map_surjective`.
351	  -- The composition-zero witness depends on the concrete definition of `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`,
352	  -- so closure here is blocked behind that definition's completion.
353	  refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
354	  · -- Sub-claim `h_zero` : `α ≫ β = 0`.
355	    -- On each affine chart `A → A' → B`, this is the local statement
356	    -- `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange A A' B ≫ KaehlerDifferential.map A A' B B = 0`,
357	    -- which follows from `KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map` (range of `mapBaseChange`
358	    -- lies in `ker map`). Globalises via presheaf morphism extensionality.
359	    sorry
360	  · -- Sub-claim `h_exact` : the short complex is exact at the middle term.
361	    -- Same ring-level input (`KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map`: range equals kernel),
362	    -- glued pointwise.
363	    sorry
364	  · -- Sub-claim `h_epi` : `β` is an epimorphism.
365	    -- Local statement: `KaehlerDifferential.map A A' B B` is surjective (Mathlib's
366	    -- `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective`). For sheaves of modules, an epimorphism can be
367	    -- verified locally on a basis of opens (mirror of `Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app`).
368	    sorry
369	
370	/-- Cotangent exact sequence for a composition of schemes `X ⟶ Y ⟶ S`.
371	
372	For `f : X ⟶ Y` and `g : Y ⟶ S`, there is an exact sequence of
373	quasi-coherent `𝒪_X`-modules
374	```
375	  f^* Ω_{Y/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y} ⟶ 0.
376	```
377	Built on affine charts from the Mathlib ring-level cotangent exact
378	sequence (`KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map`) and glued via the
379	compatibility above.
380	
381	The three sub-declarations `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`, `cotangentExactSeqBeta`,
382	and `cotangentExactSeq_structure` carry the remaining sorries; this theorem
383	itself is closed by assembling them.
384	
385	The composition-zero proof obligation is now an explicit existential
386	witness rather than an in-type `by sorry`, eliminating the iter-064/065
387	nested `by sorry` placeholder inside `ShortComplex.mk`. -/
388	theorem cotangent_exact_sequence (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
389	    ∃ (α : (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (relativeDifferentials g) ⟶
390	        relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g))
391	      (β : relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g) ⟶ relativeDifferentials f)
392	      (h : α ≫ β = 0),
393	      CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.Exact
394	        (CategoryTheory.ShortComplex.mk α β h) ∧
395	      CategoryTheory.Epi β := by
396	  obtain ⟨h, hex, hep⟩ := cotangentExactSeq_structure f g
397	  exact ⟨_, _, h, hex, hep⟩
398	
399	/-! ## Smoothness and local freeness of `Ω` -/
400	
401	/-- Smoothness of a finite-presentation morphism is equivalent to `Ω_{X/S}`
402	being locally free of the given rank.
403	
404	The forward direction is the Jacobian criterion; the converse follows from
405	the cotangent exact sequence and Nakayama's lemma applied at each point. -/
406	theorem smooth_iff_locally_free_omega (f : X ⟶ S)
407	    (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f) (n : ℕ) :
408	    Smooth f ↔
409	      ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : X.Opens), x ∈ U.1 ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
410	        let R := X.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.obj (.op U)
411	        let M := (relativeDifferentials f).val.obj (.op U)
412	        Module.Free (↑R) (↑M) ∧ Module.rank (↑R) (↑M) = n := by
413	  sorry
414	
415	/-! ## Cotangent space at a section -/
416	
417	/-- If `f : X ⟶ S` is smooth and `s : S ⟶ X` is a section of `f`, then the
418	cotangent space `s^* Ω_{X/S}` is a locally free `𝒪_S`-module of rank `n`.
419	In particular, if `S = Spec k` and `X` is smooth of relative dimension `n`,
420	the cotangent space at the corresponding `k`-point is an `n`-dimensional
421	`k`-vector space. -/
422	theorem cotangent_at_section (f : X ⟶ S)
423	    (hfp : AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFinitePresentation f)
424	    (s : S ⟶ X) (hs : s ≫ f = 𝟙 S) (n : ℕ)
425	    (hsmooth : Smooth f) :
426	    ∀ (x : S), ∃ (U : S.Opens), x ∈ U.1 ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧
427	      let R := S.ringCatSheaf.presheaf.obj (.op U)
428	      let M := ((Scheme.Modules.pullback s).obj (relativeDifferentials f)).val.obj (.op U)
429	      Module.Free (↑R) (↑M) ∧ Module.rank (↑R) (↑M) = n := by
430	  sorry
431	
432	/-! ## Restriction of scalars to k-modules -/
433	
434	/-- The `O_C(U)`-module structure on `M(U)` viewed as a `k`-module via
435	restriction of scalars along the algebra map `k → O_C(U)`. -/
436	noncomputable abbrev moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj
437	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
438	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
439	    (M : C.left.Modules)
440	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) : ModuleCat.{u} k :=
441	  (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom).obj (M.val.obj U)
442	
443	/-- Smul-naturality lemma for `moduleKPresheafOfModules`: the additive map
444	`M.val.presheaf.map f` commutes with the `k`-action on source and target after
445	restriction of scalars. -/
446	lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_smul_compat
447	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
448	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
449	    (M : C.left.Modules)
450	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
451	    M.val.presheaf.map f ≫ (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V).smul r =
452	      (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U).smul r ≫ M.val.presheaf.map f := by
453	  -- Strategy: lift to ModuleCat.smul_naturality applied to (M.val.map f) over O_C, then transport
454	  -- the smul argument from `(kToSection C U).hom r` to `(kToSection C V).hom r` using algebraMap
455	  -- naturality.
456	  have hsmul_src : (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U).smul r =
457	      (M.val.obj U).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) := rfl
458	  have hsmul_tgt : (moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V).smul r =
459	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r) := rfl
460	  rw [hsmul_src, hsmul_tgt]
461	  -- Now goal:
462	  --   M.val.presheaf.map f ≫ (M.val.obj V).smul (kV r)
463	  --     = (M.val.obj U).smul (kU r) ≫ M.val.presheaf.map f
464	  have hpres : M.val.presheaf.map f =
465	      (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat).map (M.val.map f) := by
466	    apply AddCommGrpCat.Hom.ext
467	    ext x
468	    exact PresheafOfModules.presheaf_map_apply_coe M.val f x
469	  rw [hpres]
470	  -- Now goal in terms of (forget₂).map (M.val.map f); use ModuleCat.smul_naturality:
471	  have hnat := ModuleCat.smul_naturality (M.val.map f) ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r)
472	  -- hnat has smul on the *target* of (M.val.map f) at index (kU r).
473	  -- That target is (restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (M.val.obj V), so its .smul (kU r)
474	  -- is definitionally (M.val.obj V).smul ((R.map f).hom (kU r)).
475	  -- By algebraMap_naturality, (R.map f).hom (kU r) = (kV r), which is what we need.
476	  have halg : (RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))
477	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) =
478	      (toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r := by
479	    rw [← toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_eq_kToSection, ← toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
480	    exact toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality (C := C) f r
481	  -- Convert hnat's target smul to the desired form via halg
482	  have htarget : ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars
483	        (RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))).obj (M.val.obj V)).smul
484	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r) =
485	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r) := by
486	    change (M.val.obj V).smul ((RingCat.Hom.hom (C.left.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f))
487	        ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C U).hom r)) =
488	      (M.val.obj V).smul ((toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C V).hom r)
489	    rw [halg]
490	  rw [htarget] at hnat
491	  exact hnat
492	
493	/-- The restriction map for `moduleKPresheafOfModules`, extracted as a separate
494	definition so the elaborator does not have to inline the smul-naturality proof
495	inside the main `Functor` definition. -/
496	noncomputable def moduleKPresheafOfModules_map
497	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
498	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
499	    (M : C.left.Modules)
500	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
501	    moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U ⟶ moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M V :=
502	  ModuleCat.homMk (M.val.presheaf.map f) (moduleKPresheafOfModules_smul_compat C M f)
503	
504	@[simp] lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂
505	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
506	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
507	    (M : C.left.Modules)
508	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
509	    (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map
510	        (moduleKPresheafOfModules_map C M f) = M.val.presheaf.map f := by
511	  unfold moduleKPresheafOfModules_map
512	  exact ModuleCat.forget₂_map_homMk _ _
513	
514	/-- The presheaf of `k`-modules obtained from an `O_C`-module by restriction of
515	scalars along the structure morphism `k → Γ(C, U)`.
516	
517	On each open `U`, the sections are the `O_C(U)`-module `M(U)` viewed as a
518	`k`-module via the algebra map `k → O_C(U)`. The restriction maps are `k`-linear
519	by the naturality of this algebra map. -/
520	noncomputable def moduleKPresheafOfModules
521	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
522	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
523	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
524	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
525	  obj U := moduleKPresheafOfModules_obj C M U
526	  map f := moduleKPresheafOfModules_map C M f
527	  map_id U := by
528	    apply (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map_injective
529	    rw [moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
530	    exact M.val.presheaf.map_id U
531	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
532	    apply (CategoryTheory.forget₂ (ModuleCat k) AddCommGrpCat).map_injective
533	    rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp]
534	    simp only [moduleKPresheafOfModules_map_forget₂]
535	    exact M.val.presheaf.map_comp f g
536	
537	/-- The presheaf of `k`-modules obtained by restriction of scalars is a sheaf:
538	its underlying presheaf of abelian groups coincides with that of the original
539	`O_C`-module. -/
540	lemma moduleKPresheafOfModules_isSheaf
541	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
542	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
543	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
544	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
545	      (moduleKPresheafOfModules C M) := by
546	  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_comp _ _ (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} k) AddCommGrpCat.{u})]
547	  convert M.isSheaf using 1
548	
549	/-- The sheaf of `k`-modules obtained from an `O_C`-module by restriction of
550	scalars along the structure morphism. -/
551	noncomputable def moduleKSheafOfModules
552	    {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
553	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
554	    (M : C.left.Modules) :
555	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
556	  ⟨moduleKPresheafOfModules C M, moduleKPresheafOfModules_isSheaf C M⟩
557	
558	/-! ## Serre duality genus equality -/
559	
560	/-- For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over a field `k`,
561	the dimension of `H^0(C, Ω_{C/k})` equals the dimension of `H^1(C, 𝒪_C)`,
562	and both equal the genus `g(C)`.
563	
564	This is the dimension-one case of Serre duality applied to the canonical
565	sheaf `ω_C = Ω_{C/k}`. -/
566	theorem serre_duality_genus {k : Type u} [Field k]
567	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom]
568	    (hsmooth : Smooth C.hom) :
569	    Module.rank k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0) =
570	      Module.rank k
571	        (HModule k (moduleKSheafOfModules C (relativeDifferentials C.hom)) 0) := by
572	  sorry
573	
574	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2024 Joël Riou. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Joël Riou
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Basic
10	
11	/-!
12	# The presheaf of differentials of a presheaf of modules
13	
14	In this file, we define the type `M.Derivation φ` of derivations
15	with values in a presheaf of `R`-modules `M` relative to
16	a morphism of `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` of presheaves of commutative rings
17	over categories `C` and `D` that are related by a functor `F : C ⥤ D`.
18	We formalize the notion of universal derivation.
19	
20	Geometrically, if `f : X ⟶ S` is a morphism of schemes (or more generally
21	a morphism of commutative ringed spaces), we would like to apply
22	these definitions in the case where `F` is the pullback functor from
23	open subsets of `S` to open subsets of `X` and `φ` is the
24	morphism $O_S ⟶ f_* O_X$.
25	
26	In order to prove that there exists a universal derivation, the target
27	of which shall be called the presheaf of relative differentials of `φ`,
28	we first study the case where `F` is the identity functor.
29	In this case where we have a morphism of presheaves of commutative
30	rings `φ' : S' ⟶ R`, we construct a derivation
31	`DifferentialsConstruction.derivation'` which is universal.
32	Then, the general case (TODO) shall be obtained by observing that
33	derivations for `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` identify to derivations
34	for `S' ⟶ R` where `S'` is the pullback by `F` of the presheaf of
35	commutative rings `S` (the data is the same: it suffices
36	to show that the two vanishing conditions `d_app` are equivalent).
37	
38	-/
39	
40	@[expose] public section
41	
42	universe v u v₁ v₂ u₁ u₂
43	
44	open CategoryTheory
45	
46	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
47	
48	namespace PresheafOfModules
49	
50	variable {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}} {F : C ⥤ D} {S' R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
51	  (M N : PresheafOfModules.{v} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
52	  (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) (φ' : S' ⟶ R)
53	
54	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`,
55	this is the type of relative `φ`-derivation of a presheaf of `R`-modules `M`. -/
56	@[ext]
57	structure Derivation where
58	  /-- the underlying additive map `R.obj X →+ M.obj X` of a derivation -/
59	  d {X : Dᵒᵖ} : R.obj X →+ M.obj X
60	  d_mul {X : Dᵒᵖ} (a b : R.obj X) : d (a * b) = a • d b + b • d a := by cat_disch
61	  d_map {X Y : Dᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) (x : R.obj X) :
62	    d (R.map f x) = M.map f (d x) := by cat_disch
63	  d_app {X : Cᵒᵖ} (a : S.obj X) : d (φ.app X a) = 0 := by cat_disch
64	
65	namespace Derivation
66	
67	-- Note: `d_app` cannot be a simp lemma because `dsimp` would
68	-- simplify the composition of functors `R ⋙ forget₂ _ _`
69	attribute [simp] d_mul d_map
70	
71	variable {M N φ}
72	
73	lemma congr_d {d d' : M.Derivation φ} (h : d = d') {X : Dᵒᵖ} (b : R.obj X) :
74	    d.d b = d'.d b := by rw [h]
75	
76	variable (d : M.Derivation φ)
77	
78	@[simp] lemma d_one (X : Dᵒᵖ) : d.d (X := X) 1 = 0 := by
79	  simpa using d.d_mul (X := X) 1 1
80	
81	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
82	/-- The postcomposition of a derivation by a morphism of presheaves of modules. -/
83	@[simps! d_apply]
84	def postcomp (f : M ⟶ N) : N.Derivation φ where
85	  d := (f.app _).hom.toAddMonoidHom.comp d.d
86	  d_map {X Y} g x := by simpa using naturality_apply f g (d.d x)
87	  d_app {X} a := by
88	    dsimp
89	    erw [d_app]
90	    rw [map_zero]
91	
92	/-- The universal property that a derivation `d : M.Derivation φ` must
93	satisfy so that the presheaf of modules `M` can be considered as the presheaf of
94	(relative) differentials of a presheaf of commutative rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`. -/
95	structure Universal where
96	  /-- An absolute derivation of `M'` descends as a morphism `M ⟶ M'`. -/
97	  desc {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
98	    (d' : M'.Derivation φ) : M ⟶ M'
99	  fac {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
100	    (d' : M'.Derivation φ) : d.postcomp (desc d') = d' := by cat_disch
101	  postcomp_injective {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
102	    (φ φ' : M ⟶ M') (h : d.postcomp φ = d.postcomp φ') : φ = φ' := by cat_disch
103	
104	attribute [simp] Universal.fac
105	
106	instance : Subsingleton d.Universal where
107	  allEq h₁ h₂ := by
108	    suffices ∀ {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)}
109	      (d' : M'.Derivation φ), h₁.desc d' = h₂.desc d' by
110	        cases h₁
111	        cases h₂
112	        simp only [Universal.mk.injEq]
113	        ext : 2
114	        apply this
115	    intro M' d'
116	    apply h₁.postcomp_injective
117	    simp
118	
119	end Derivation
120	
121	/-- The property that there exists a universal derivation for
122	a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`. -/
123	class HasDifferentials : Prop where
124	  exists_universal_derivation : ∃ (M : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
125	      (d : M.Derivation φ), Nonempty d.Universal
126	
127	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings `φ : S ⟶ R`,
128	this is the type of relative `φ`-derivation of a presheaf of `R`-modules `M`. -/
129	abbrev Derivation' : Type _ := M.Derivation (F := 𝟭 D) φ'
130	
131	namespace Derivation'
132	
133	variable {M φ'}
134	
135	@[simp]
136	lemma d_app (d : M.Derivation' φ') {X : Dᵒᵖ} (a : S'.obj X) :
137	    d.d (φ'.app X a) = 0 :=
138	  Derivation.d_app d _
139	
140	/-- The derivation relative to the morphism of commutative rings `φ'.app X` induced by
141	a derivation relative to a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings. -/
142	noncomputable def app (d : M.Derivation' φ') (X : Dᵒᵖ) : (M.obj X).Derivation (φ'.app X) :=
143	  ModuleCat.Derivation.mk (fun b ↦ d.d b)
144	
145	@[simp]
146	lemma app_apply (d : M.Derivation' φ') {X : Dᵒᵖ} (b : R.obj X) :
147	    (d.app X).d b = d.d b := rfl
148	
149	section
150	
151	variable (d : ∀ (X : Dᵒᵖ), (M.obj X).Derivation (φ'.app X))
152	
153	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
154	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of commutative rings `φ'`, this is the
155	in derivation `M.Derivation' φ'` that is given by a compatible family of derivations
156	with values in the modules `M.obj X` for all `X`. -/
157	def mk (d_map : ∀ ⦃X Y : Dᵒᵖ⦄ (f : X ⟶ Y) (x : R.obj X),
158	    (d Y).d ((R.map f) x) = (M.map f) ((d X).d x)) : M.Derivation' φ' where
159	  d {X} := AddMonoidHom.mk' (d X).d (by simp)
160	
161	variable (d_map : ∀ ⦃X Y : Dᵒᵖ⦄ (f : X ⟶ Y) (x : R.obj X),
162	      (d Y).d ((R.map f) x) = (M.map f) ((d X).d x))
163	
164	@[simp]
165	lemma mk_app (X : Dᵒᵖ) : (mk d d_map).app X = d X := rfl
166	
167	/-- Constructor for `Derivation.Universal` in the case `F` is the identity functor. -/
168	def Universal.mk {d : M.Derivation' φ'}
169	    (desc : ∀ {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)}
170	      (_ : M'.Derivation' φ'), M ⟶ M')
171	    (fac : ∀ {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)}
172	      (d' : M'.Derivation' φ'), d.postcomp (desc d') = d')
173	    (postcomp_injective : ∀ {M' : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)}
174	      (α β : M ⟶ M'), d.postcomp α = d.postcomp β → α = β) : d.Universal where
175	  desc := desc
176	  fac := fac
177	  postcomp_injective := postcomp_injective
178	
179	end
180	
181	end Derivation'
182	
183	namespace DifferentialsConstruction
184	
185	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
186	/-- The presheaf of relative differentials of a morphism of presheaves of
187	commutative rings. -/
188	@[simps -isSimp]
189	noncomputable def relativeDifferentials' :
190	    PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _) where
191	  obj X := CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (φ'.app X)
192	  -- Have to hint `g' := R.map f` below, or it gets unfolded weirdly.
193	  map f := CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.map (g' := R.map f) (φ'.naturality f)
194	  -- Without `dsimp`, `ext` doesn't pick up the right lemmas.
195	  map_id _ := by dsimp; ext; simp
196	  map_comp _ _ := by dsimp; ext; simp
197	
198	attribute [simp] relativeDifferentials'_obj
199	
200	@[simp]
201	lemma relativeDifferentials'_map_d {X Y : Dᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) (x : R.obj X) :
202	    DFunLike.coe (α := CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (φ'.app X))
203	      (β := fun _ ↦ CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential (φ'.app Y))
204	      (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (R := ↑(R.obj X)) ((relativeDifferentials' φ').map f))
205	        (CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.d x) =
206	        CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.d (R.map f x) :=
207	  CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.map_d (φ'.naturality f) _
208	
209	/-- The universal derivation. -/
210	noncomputable def derivation' : (relativeDifferentials' φ').Derivation' φ' :=
211	  Derivation'.mk (fun X ↦ CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.D (φ'.app X))
212	    (fun _ _ f x ↦ (relativeDifferentials'_map_d φ' f x).symm)
213	
214	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
215	/-- The derivation `Derivation' φ'` is universal. -/
216	noncomputable def isUniversal' : (derivation' φ').Universal :=
217	  Derivation'.Universal.mk
218	    (fun {M'} d' ↦
219	      { app := fun X ↦ (d'.app X).desc
220	        naturality := fun {X Y} f ↦ CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.ext (fun b ↦ by
221	          dsimp
222	          rw [ModuleCat.Derivation.desc_d, Derivation'.app_apply]
223	          erw [relativeDifferentials'_map_d φ' f]
224	          simp) })
225	    (fun {M'} d' ↦ by
226	      ext X b
227	      apply ModuleCat.Derivation.desc_d)
228	    (fun {M} α β h ↦ by
229	      ext1 X
230	      exact CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential.ext (Derivation.congr_d h))
231	
232	instance : HasDifferentials (F := 𝟭 D) φ' := ⟨_, _, ⟨isUniversal' φ'⟩⟩
233	
234	end DifferentialsConstruction
235	
236	end PresheafOfModules
237

56:/-- The pushforward functor on presheaves of modules for a functor `F : C ⥤ D` and
59:def pushforward₀ (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
61:  obj M := pushforward₀_obj F R M
64:/-- If `F : C ⥤ D` if a functor and `R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat` is a presheaf
65:of commutative rings, this is the pushforward functor from the category

106:        (((pushforward φ).obj M).map f).hom m = M.map (F.map f.unop).op m := rfl

10	/-!
11	# Pushforward of presheaves of modules
12	
13	If `F : C ⥤ D`, the precomposition `F.op ⋙ _` induces a functor from presheaves
14	over `D` to presheaves over `C`. When `R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat`, we define the
15	induced functor `pushforward₀ : PresheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} (F.op ⋙ R)`
16	on presheaves of modules.
17	
18	In case we have a morphism of presheaves of rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, we also construct
19	a functor `pushforward : PresheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} S`, and
20	we show that they interact with the composition of morphisms similarly as pseudofunctors.
21	
22	-/
23	
24	@[expose] public section
25	
26	universe v v₁ v₂ v₃ v₄ u₁ u₂ u₃ u₄ u
27	
28	open CategoryTheory Functor
29	
30	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
31	  {E : Type u₃} [Category.{v₃} E] {E' : Type u₄} [Category.{v₄} E']
32	
33	namespace PresheafOfModules
34	
35	variable (F : C ⥤ D)
36	
37	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
38	/-- Implementation of `pushforward₀`. -/
39	@[simps]
40	def pushforward₀_obj (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) (M : PresheafOfModules R) :
41	    PresheafOfModules (F.op ⋙ R) :=
42	  { obj X := ModuleCat.of _ (M.obj (F.op.obj X))
43	    map {X Y} f := M.map (F.op.map f)
44	    map_id X := by
45	      refine ModuleCat.hom_ext
46	        -- Work around an instance diamond for `restrictScalarsId'`
47	        (@LinearMap.ext _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (_) (_) _ _ _ (fun x => ?_))
48	      exact (M.congr_map_apply (F.op.map_id X) x).trans (by simp)
49	    map_comp := fun f g ↦ by
50	      refine ModuleCat.hom_ext
51	        -- Work around an instance diamond for `restrictScalarsId'`
52	        (@LinearMap.ext _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (_) (_) _ _ _ (fun x => ?_))
53	      exact (M.congr_map_apply (F.op.map_comp f g) x).trans (by simp) }
54	
55	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
56	/-- The pushforward functor on presheaves of modules for a functor `F : C ⥤ D` and
57	`R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat`. On the underlying presheaves of abelian groups, it is induced
58	by the precomposition with `F.op`. -/
59	def pushforward₀ (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
60	    PresheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} (F.op ⋙ R) where
61	  obj M := pushforward₀_obj F R M
62	  map {M₁ M₂} φ := { app X := φ.app _ }
63	
64	/-- If `F : C ⥤ D` if a functor and `R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat` is a presheaf
65	of commutative rings, this is the pushforward functor from the category
66	of presheaves of modules on `R` to the category of presheaves of
67	modules on `F.op ⋙ R`. -/
68	abbrev pushforward₀OfCommRingCat (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}) :
69	    PresheafOfModules.{v} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _) ⥤
70	      PresheafOfModules.{v} ((F.op ⋙ R) ⋙ forget₂ _ _) :=
71	  pushforward₀ F (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)
72	
73	/-- The pushforward of presheaves of modules commutes with the forgetful functor
74	to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
75	noncomputable def pushforward₀CompToPresheaf (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
76	    pushforward₀.{v} F R ⋙ toPresheaf _ ≅ toPresheaf _ ⋙ (whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj F.op :=
77	  Iso.refl _

87	  pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ

92	    pushforward.{v} φ ⋙ toPresheaf _ ≅ toPresheaf _ ⋙ (whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj F.op :=
93	  Iso.refl _

110	    (((pushforward φ).map α).app X).hom m = α.app (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)) m := rfl

113	@[simp]

115	    (m : (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app X).hom).obj (M.obj (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)))) :
116	    DFunLike.coe
117	      (F := ↑((ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).obj _) →ₗ[_] ↑((ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).obj _))

12	/-!
13	# The category of presheaves of modules over a scheme
14	
15	In this file, given a scheme `X`, we define the category of presheaves
16	of modules over `X`. As categories of presheaves of modules are
17	defined for presheaves of rings (and not presheaves of commutative rings),
18	we also introduce a definition `X.ringCatSheaf` for the underlying sheaf
19	of rings of `X`.

33	/-- The underlying sheaf of rings of a scheme. -/
34	abbrev ringCatSheaf : TopCat.Sheaf RingCat.{u} X :=
35	  (sheafCompose _ (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat.{u})).obj X.sheaf
36	
37	/-- The category of presheaves of modules over a scheme. -/
38	nonrec abbrev PresheafOfModules := PresheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf.obj
39

43	    Y.ringCatSheaf ⟶ ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).sheafPushforwardContinuous

import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf
import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Presheaf
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Presheaf

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry

universe u

variable {X Y S : Scheme.{u}}

noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
    f.c
  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'

example (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
    let φ_g' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat g.base).homEquiv
      S.presheaf Y.presheaf).symm g.c
    let φ_fg' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).homEquiv
      S.presheaf X.presheaf).symm (f ≫ g).c
    let M : Y.PresheafOfModules := (PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj
      (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g))
    M.Derivation' φ_g' := by
  intro φ_g' φ_fg' M
  let D_X := PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_fg'
  refine {
    d := fun {U} => AddMonoidHom.mk' (fun b => D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom b)) ?_,
    d_mul := ?_,
    d_map := ?_,
    d_app := ?_
  }
  · intro a b
    simp
  · intro U a b
    dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
    rw [(f.c.app U).hom.map_mul, D_X.d_mul]
    rfl
  · intro U V f' x
    dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
    -- Goal: D_X.d ((f.c.app V).hom (Y.presheaf.map f' x))
    --     = M.map f' (D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom x))
    have hnat : (Y.presheaf.map f') ≫ (f.c.app V) = (f.c.app U) ≫
        ((TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).obj X.presheaf).map f' := f.c.naturality f'
    have hnat' : (f.c.app V).hom (Y.presheaf.map f' x) =
        X.presheaf.map ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).map f'.unop).op
          ((f.c.app U).hom x) := by
      have := congr_arg (fun h => ConcreteCategory.hom h x) hnat
      simpa using this
    rw [hnat']
    exact (D_X.d_map _ _).symm
  · intro U a
    sorry

variable {X Y S : Scheme.{u}}

example (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :

import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf
import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Presheaf
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Presheaf

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry

universe u

variable {X Y S : Scheme.{u}}

noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
    f.c
  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'

example (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
    let φ_g' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat g.base).homEquiv
      S.presheaf Y.presheaf).symm g.c
    let φ_fg' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).homEquiv
      S.presheaf X.presheaf).symm (f ≫ g).c
    let M : Y.PresheafOfModules := (PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj
      (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g))
    M.Derivation' φ_g' := by
  intro φ_g' φ_fg' M
  let D_X := PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_fg'
  let adj_f := TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base
  -- Key identity: φ_g' ≫ f.c = adj_f.unit.app(_) ≫ (pushforward f.base).map φ_fg' (by rfl)
  refine {
    d := fun {U} => AddMonoidHom.mk' (fun b => D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom b)) ?_,
    d_mul := ?_,
    d_map := ?_,
    d_app := ?_
  }
  · intro a b
    simp
  · intro U a b
    dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
    rw [(f.c.app U).hom.map_mul, D_X.d_mul]
    rfl
  · intro U V f' x
    dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
    have hnat : (Y.presheaf.map f') ≫ (f.c.app V) = (f.c.app U) ≫
        ((TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).obj X.presheaf).map f' := f.c.naturality f'
    have hnat' : (f.c.app V).hom (Y.presheaf.map f' x) =
        X.presheaf.map ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).map f'.unop).op
          ((f.c.app U).hom x) := by
      have := congr_arg (fun h => ConcreteCategory.hom h x) hnat
      simpa using this
    rw [hnat']
    exact (D_X.d_map _ _).symm
  · intro U a
    dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
    -- Key identity used here:
    have hcomm : φ_g' ≫ f.c = (adj_f.unit.app ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat g.base).obj S.presheaf)) ≫
        (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ_fg' := rfl
    -- Apply to a
    have hcomm_app : (f.c.app U).hom (φ_g'.app U a) =
        (φ_fg'.app (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))).hom
          ((adj_f.unit.app ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat g.base).obj S.presheaf)).app U a) := by
      have h1 := congr_arg (fun h => ConcreteCategory.hom (NatTrans.app h U) a) hcomm
      simpa using h1
    rw [hcomm_app]
    -- Now apply D_X.d_app
    exact D_X.d_app _

216	  -- Set up the two ring presheaf morphisms that drive the relativeDifferentials' construction.

236	  -- Substep (i): produce the morphism of presheaves of Y-modules.

import Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic

import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf

set_option autoImplicit false
universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

variable {X Y S : Scheme.{u}}

noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
    f.c
  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'

theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf (f : X ⟶ S) :
    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat)
      (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf := sorry

noncomputable def relativeDifferentials (f : X ⟶ S) : X.Modules :=
  ⟨relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f, relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf f⟩

noncomputable def cotangentExactSeqAlpha (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (relativeDifferentials g) ⟶
      relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g) := by
  refine ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _).symm ?_
  let φ_g' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat g.base).homEquiv
    S.presheaf Y.presheaf).symm g.c
  let φ_fg' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).homEquiv
    S.presheaf X.presheaf).symm (f ≫ g).c
  let D_X := PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_fg'
  let adj_f := TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base
  let d_target :
      ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj
        (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g))).Derivation' φ_g' :=
    { d := fun {U} => AddMonoidHom.mk' (fun b => D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom b)) (by intro a b; simp)
      d_mul := by
        intro U a b
        dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
        rw [(f.c.app U).hom.map_mul, D_X.d_mul]
        rfl
      d_map := by
        intro U V f' x
        dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
        have hnat' : (f.c.app V).hom (Y.presheaf.map f' x) =
            X.presheaf.map ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).map f'.unop).op
              ((f.c.app U).hom x) := by
          have := congr_arg (fun h => ConcreteCategory.hom h x) (f.c.naturality f')
          simpa using this
        rw [hnat']
        exact (D_X.d_map _ _).symm
      d_app := by
        intro U a
        dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
        have hcomm : φ_g' ≫ f.c =
            (adj_f.unit.app ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat g.base).obj S.presheaf)) ≫
              (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ_fg' := rfl
        have hcomm_app : (f.c.app U).hom (φ_g'.app U a) =
            (φ_fg'.app (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))).hom
              ((adj_f.unit.app ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat g.base).obj S.presheaf)).app
                U a) := by
          have h1 := congr_arg (fun h => ConcreteCategory.hom (NatTrans.app h U) a) hcomm
          simpa using h1
        rw [hcomm_app]
        exact D_X.d_app _ }
  let presheafHom : relativeDifferentialsPresheaf g ⟶
      (PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj
        (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g)) :=
    (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').desc d_target
  exact ⟨presheafHom⟩

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

230	  -- derivation laws on `D_X`.
231	  -- The vanishing condition `d (φ_g'.app U a) = 0` follows from the adjunction-coherence
232	  -- identity `(f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom.app U) ∘ (φ_g'.app U)
233	  --   = (φ_fg'.app (f⁻¹U)) ∘ (pullback-naturality map)`
234	  -- (an instance of the `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction` naturality across the composition
235	  -- `f ≫ g`), combined with `D_X.d_app`.
236	  -- Substep (i): produce the morphism of presheaves of Y-modules.
237	  -- This is the substantive sorry; the decomposition above splits naturally into four named
238	  -- subgoals (`d`, `d_mul`, `d_map`, `d_app`) once the `Derivation'` constructor is unfolded.
239	  let D_X := PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_fg'
240	  let adj_f := TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base
241	  let d_target :
242	      ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj
243	        (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g))).Derivation' φ_g' :=
244	    { d := fun {U} => AddMonoidHom.mk' (fun b => D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom b)) (by intro a b; simp)
245	      d_mul := by
246	        intro U a b
247	        dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
248	        rw [(f.c.app U).hom.map_mul, D_X.d_mul]
249	        rfl
250	      d_map := by
251	        intro U V f' x
252	        dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
253	        have hnat' : (f.c.app V).hom (Y.presheaf.map f' x) =
254	            X.presheaf.map ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).map f'.unop).op
255	              ((f.c.app U).hom x) := by
256	          have := congr_arg (fun h => ConcreteCategory.hom h x) (f.c.naturality f')
257	          simpa using this
258	        rw [hnat']
259	        exact (D_X.d_map _ _).symm
260	      d_app := by
261	        intro U a
262	        dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
263	        -- Adjunction-coherence: `φ_g' ≫ f.c = adj_f.unit ≫ (pushforward f).map φ_fg'` (rfl).
264	        have hcomm : φ_g' ≫ f.c =
265	            (adj_f.unit.app ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat g.base).obj S.presheaf)) ≫
266	              (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ_fg' := rfl
267	        have hcomm_app : (f.c.app U).hom (φ_g'.app U a) =
268	            (φ_fg'.app (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))).hom
269	              ((adj_f.unit.app ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat g.base).obj S.presheaf)).app
270	                U a) := by
271	          have h1 := congr_arg (fun h => ConcreteCategory.hom (NatTrans.app h U) a) hcomm
272	          simpa using h1
273	        rw [hcomm_app]
274	        exact D_X.d_app _ }
275	  let presheafHom : relativeDifferentialsPresheaf g ⟶
276	      (PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj
277	        (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g)) :=
278	    (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').desc d_target
279	  exact ⟨presheafHom⟩
280	
281	/-- The relative-quotient cotangent map `Ω_{X/S} ⟶ Ω_{X/Y}`.

import Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.FinitePresentation
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth
import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Presheaf
import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Presheaf
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf

set_option autoImplicit false

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

variable {X Y S : Scheme.{u}}

noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
    f.c
  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'

theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf (f : X ⟶ S) :
    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat)
      (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf := by sorry

noncomputable def relativeDifferentials (f : X ⟶ S) : X.Modules :=
  ⟨relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f, relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf f⟩

-- Test that the same construction works end-to-end as in the file.
noncomputable def cotangentExactSeqAlpha (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ S) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pullback f).obj (relativeDifferentials g) ⟶
      relativeDifferentials (f ≫ g) := by
  refine ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).homEquiv _ _).symm ?_
  let φ_g' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat g.base).homEquiv
    S.presheaf Y.presheaf).symm g.c
  let φ_fg' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat (f ≫ g).base).homEquiv
    S.presheaf X.presheaf).symm (f ≫ g).c
  let D_X := PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.derivation' φ_fg'
  let adj_f := TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base
  let d_target :
      ((PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj
        (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g))).Derivation' φ_g' :=
    { d := fun {U} => AddMonoidHom.mk' (fun b => D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom b)) (by intro a b; simp)
      d_mul := by
        intro U a b
        dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
        rw [(f.c.app U).hom.map_mul, D_X.d_mul]
        rfl
      d_map := by
        intro U V f' x
        dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
        have hnat' : (f.c.app V).hom (Y.presheaf.map f' x) =
            X.presheaf.map ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).map f'.unop).op
              ((f.c.app U).hom x) := by
          have := congr_arg (fun h => ConcreteCategory.hom h x) (f.c.naturality f')
          simpa using this
        rw [hnat']
        exact (D_X.d_map _ _).symm
      d_app := by
        intro U a
        dsimp only [AddMonoidHom.mk', AddMonoidHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
        have hcomm : φ_g' ≫ f.c =
            (adj_f.unit.app ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat g.base).obj S.presheaf)) ≫
              (TopCat.Presheaf.pushforward CommRingCat f.base).map φ_fg' := rfl
        have hcomm_app : (f.c.app U).hom (φ_g'.app U a) =
            (φ_fg'.app (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map f.base).obj U.unop))).hom
              ((adj_f.unit.app ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat g.base).obj S.presheaf)).app
                U a) := by
          have h1 := congr_arg (fun h => ConcreteCategory.hom (NatTrans.app h U) a) hcomm
          simpa using h1
        rw [hcomm_app]
        exact D_X.d_app _ }
  let presheafHom : relativeDifferentialsPresheaf g ⟶
      (PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).obj
        (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f ≫ g)) :=
    (PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.isUniversal' φ_g').desc d_target
  exact ⟨presheafHom⟩

#check @cotangentExactSeqAlpha

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
